method_steps: Heat olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic and cook for 3 minutes until softened.
Increase heat to high, add beef mince and cook, breaking it apart, until browned.
Add red wine and simmer for 1 minute, scraping the bottom of the pot.

Add crushed tomato, tomato paste, sugar, Worcestershire sauce, bay leaves, thyme, bouillon cubes, salt and pepper. Stir, bring to a simmer, then reduce heat to medium and cook for 20-30 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt as needed.
Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente, then serve topped with the bolognese sauce, parmesan and parsley.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat; cook garlic and onion for 2 minutes until golden.
Add beef and cook for 2 minutes, breaking it up, until light brown, then stir in the garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, cumin, paprika, cayenne, salt and pepper and cook for 2 minutes.
Add tomato paste and water; cook for 1 minute until the liquid mostly evaporates.
Place taco shells in a baking pan, fill with the meat mixture and top with shredded cheese.
Bake for 5-7 minutes until the cheese melts and the shells are crisp.
Serve hot with lettuce, tomato, onion, sour cream and taco sauce.
Heat oil over medium-high heat. Add garlic and onion, cook 1 minute, then add capsicum and cook 2 minutes until onion is translucent.
Increase heat to high, add beef mince and cook, breaking it apart, until mostly browned.
Add the chilli spice mix (cayenne, paprika, cumin, garlic powder, onion powder, oregano, salt) and cook until the beef is completely browned.
Add tomato paste, crushed tomato, kidney beans, bouillon cubes, sugar and water. Simmer gently for 20-40 minutes on medium-low, uncovered.
Adjust salt to taste before serving.
Serve over rice or with corn chips and toppings such as sour cream, cheese and coriander.

3.new_title: Slow Cooked Shredded Beef Ragu Pasta (RTE)
method_steps: Pat the beef dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over high heat. Sear the beef on all sides until browned, 3-5 minutes, then remove to a plate.
Reduce heat to medium-low, add remaining oil, then sauté garlic and onion for 2 minutes. Add carrot and celery and cook slowly for 5 minutes.

Add crushed tomato, tomato paste, bouillon cubes, red wine, water, thyme and bay leaves. Return the beef to the pot, bring to a simmer, then reduce to low and cook covered for 2 hours until the beef is tender.
Remove the beef and shred with two forks, then return to the pot and simmer for 30 minutes until the sauce reduces and thickens.
Taste and adjust seasoning with salt, pepper and a little sugar if needed.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the pappardelle 1 minute less than the packet instructions.
Heat 5 cups of the ragu in a large pan over high heat while the pasta cooks.
Transfer the pasta directly to the pan of ragu using tongs, adding 3/4 cup of the pasta cooking water.
Toss gently for 1-2 minutes until the sauce coats the pasta thickly.
Serve immediately with plenty of grated parmesan and parsley.
Mix cornstarch, water, sugar, dark and light soy sauce, Chinese cooking wine, five spice, sesame oil and pepper in a bowl to make the sauce.
Slice the beef thinly and combine with 2 tablespoons of the sauce; set aside.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat.
Add beef, spread out and leave for 1 minute until browned, then stir for 10 seconds and add garlic and ginger, stirring 30 seconds until beef is no longer pink.
Pour in the remaining sauce and water; mix quickly.
When the sauce bubbles, add the broccoli and stir to coat, simmering for 1 minute until thickened.
Remove from heat immediately and serve over rice, sprinkled with sesame seeds if desired.

5.new_title: Beef Chow Mein - great mince/ground beef recipe! (RTE)
method_steps: Mix soy sauces, oyster sauce, cooking wine, sugar and white pepper in a bowl to make the chow mein sauce.
Mix cornflour and water in a separate bowl for the thickener.
Prepare the noodles according to packet directions; drain.
Heat oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat and cook garlic for 10 seconds.
Add beef mince, breaking it apart, until no pink remains.
Add the white parts of the green onions and 1.5 tbsp of the sauce; cook 2 minutes until caramelised.
Mix the cornflour slurry into the remaining sauce.
Add egg to the pan, scrambling it through the beef mixture, then add carrot and cabbage and cook 1 minute until wilted.
Add bean sprouts, noodles and sauce; toss for 1 minute until dispersed.
Add the green parts of the onions and toss for another minute, then serve.
Grate the onion into a large bowl until about 1/2 cup juice accumulates, then mix in the diced bread until soaked and disintegrated, about 5 minutes.
Add the ground beef, ground pork, egg, parsley, garlic, parmigiano reggiano, salt and pepper and combine thoroughly by hand.
Measure heaped tablespoon portions and roll into balls.
Heat 1.5 tbsp olive oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat and brown the meatballs on all sides, 3-4 minutes.
Transfer the browned meatballs to a plate.
Add the remaining 1 tbsp olive oil to the pan and sauté the onion and garlic for 2-3 minutes until translucent.
Add the tomato passata, water, red pepper flakes, Italian herb mix, salt and pepper and bring to a simmer, then reduce to medium-low.
Return the meatballs and pooled juices to the sauce and cook for 8-10 minutes, stirring occasionally; adjust seasoning.
Cook pasta and serve the meatballs over pasta with extra cheese and parsley.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat; add garlic and onion and cook until translucent.
Add beef, breaking it up while cooking; once browned, stir in the seasoning, tomato paste and water and cook 2 minutes more.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Spread half the corn chips in a single layer on a baking tray, drizzle with about 1 cup of the cheese sauce, then top with half the beef and grated cheese.
Add the remaining chips, sauce, beef and cheese.
Bake for 15 minutes until the cheese melts.
Serve immediately with guacamole, pico de gallo, scallions and sour cream on the side.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic and cook 1 minute, then add carrot and celery and cook 3 minutes until softened.
Increase heat to high and add beef mince, breaking it apart as it browns.
Stir in flour, then tomato paste, beef stock, red wine, bouillon cube, Worcestershire sauce, thyme, bay leaves, salt and pepper.
Simmer on medium-high for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until reduced to a gravy consistency. Taste and adjust salt.
Transfer to a 6-cup pie dish and cool if possible.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Cook potatoes in boiling water for 15 minutes until soft, drain and steam dry for 30 seconds.
Add butter and mash until melted, then add milk and salt and mash until smooth.
Spread the mash onto the filling, rough up the surface with a fork, sprinkle with parmesan and drizzle with butter.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden and bubbling at the edges.
Stand for 5 minutes before serving.
Flatten the steaks to 3/4cm thickness, slice into 5mm strips and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet over high heat. Scatter half the beef, spread out and leave for 30 seconds until browned, flip and cook 30 seconds, then remove to a plate.
Add the remaining oil and repeat with the remaining beef.
Reduce heat to medium-high, add butter and melt, then add onions and cook 1 minute, followed by the mushrooms.
Cook the mushrooms until golden, scraping up the browned bits from the pan.
Add flour and cook, stirring, for 1 minute.
Add half the beef broth while stirring, then once incorporated add the remaining broth.
Stir in the sour cream and mustard until combined.
Bring to a simmer and reduce heat to medium-low. Once thickened to a pouring cream consistency, 3-5 minutes, adjust seasoning.
Add the beef back with the plate juices, simmer 1 minute, then remove from heat immediately.
Serve over pasta or noodles with chives.
Combine all rissole ingredients (beef mince, grated onion, panko, zucchini, carrot, egg, garlic, thyme, oregano, pepper, salt and Worcestershire sauce) by hand.
Form into 12 patties, indenting one side to prevent doming.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large non-stick pan and cook half the rissoles for 1.5 minutes per side until lightly browned but raw inside. Repeat with remaining oil and rissoles.
Wipe the pan clean, melt the butter, cook the onion for 1 minute, then the mushrooms for 3-4 minutes until softened, adding garlic for the final 30 seconds.
Reduce heat to medium, scatter the flour across the pan and stir for 1 minute until a dry paste forms.
Slowly pour in half the stock while stirring until the flour dissolves and thickens, then add the remaining stock.
Add Worcestershire sauce, salt and pepper, stir, and increase heat to medium-high.
When simmering, gently place the rissoles in the pan with any pooled juices and cook 4-5 minutes until the gravy thickens.
Serve over mashed potato with plenty of sauce.
Remove the lemongrass outer layers, reserving trimmings for the beef, and finely chop the white part for the paste.
Place the beef, broth, bay leaves and lemongrass trimmings in a saucepan and simmer for 1.5-2 hours until fork tender. Remove the beef and reduce the liquid to about 1.5 cups.

Char the eschallot, garlic and galangal in a hot skillet for about 1.5 minutes, then char the dried chillies 10 seconds per side. Cool, then grate the galangal, peel the garlic and remove the chilli seeds.
Toast the dried spices (cloves, cumin, nutmeg, coriander, cardamom, cinnamon) for 30 seconds until fragrant.
Blitz the chillies in a food processor, then add the galangal, lemongrass, toasted spices and remaining paste ingredients with water, blitzing until smooth.
Heat oil in a pot over medium-high heat, add the curry paste and cook for 3 minutes until reduced and thickened.
Add the coconut milk and stir well.
Add the cinnamon quill, star anise and reserved beef braising liquid and simmer for 3 minutes.
Stir in the fish sauce, tamarind and sugar.
Add the potatoes and cook for 7 minutes until tender.
Add the beef and simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning to taste.
Serve over jasmine rice, garnished with peanuts, fried shallots and fresh chillies.
Divide the beef into 4 portions and lightly form into patties matching the bun size, about 10cm, without pressing hard.
Season generously with salt and pepper on both sides, creating a dent on one side to prevent doming.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a heavy skillet over high heat, add the onions and cook until wilted and caramelised, then season and remove.
Heat the remaining oil until smoking, add the patties and cook 2 minutes for a deep golden crust, flip, cook 1 minute, top with cheese if desired, cover and cook 1 more minute.
Toast the cut sides of the buns lightly.
Layer the buns with condiments, lettuce, tomato, patty, caramelised onions and pickles, and serve with chips.
Combine the beef with the marinade ingredients (soy sauce, cornstarch, oil) and marinate for 10 minutes.
Mix the cornflour with a splash of water, then add the remaining sauce ingredients (soy sauce, chicken broth, cooking wine, brown sugar).
Coat the beef lightly with cornstarch using your fingers.
Heat oil in a wok over medium-high heat and cook half the beef for about 45 seconds per side until golden and crisp. Transfer to a paper-towel-lined plate and repeat with the remaining beef.
Discard the oil, leaving 1 tablespoon in the wok.
Sauté the ginger and garlic for 15 seconds.
Add the sauce and simmer for about 1.5 minutes until thickened into a glossy coating.
Add the beef and scallions, toss and cook for 30 seconds, then serve immediately with rice.
Combine sauce ingredients in a small bowl and set aside.
Heat sesame oil in a large wok over medium-high heat, add the whisked eggs and stir gently until softly scrambled, then transfer to a plate.

Heat the vegetable oil in the same pan over high heat, add onion and garlic and cook 30 seconds, then add the ground beef, breaking it up, and cook 2 minutes until no raw meat remains. Add 2 tablespoons of the sauce and cook 1 minute longer.
Add the frozen vegetables and cook 2 minutes until the beef caramelises.
Add the rice and remaining sauce, tossing for 2 minutes until the sauce disperses and the rice begins to caramelise.
Add the scrambled egg and green onions, toss briefly and serve immediately.
Mix together the spice mix ingredients (onion powder, garlic powder, cumin, paprika, oregano, cayenne) and set aside.
For the sauce, heat oil in a large saucepan over medium heat, add flour, stir into a paste and cook 1 minute.
Add half the chicken stock and whisk until a smooth paste forms, then add the remaining stock.

Add the passata, salt, pepper and 2 tablespoons of the spice mix, whisk to combine, then increase heat to medium-high and cook 3-5 minutes, whisking regularly, until the sauce reaches a thick syrup consistency. Remove from heat.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat, add garlic and onion and cook 2 minutes.
Add beef and cook 2 minutes, breaking it up, then add the remaining spice mix and cook 2 more minutes until completely browned.
Stir in the refried beans, black beans, 1/4 cup enchilada sauce, salt and pepper and cook 2 minutes, then remove from heat.
Smear a little sauce on the bottom of a baking dish to prevent sliding.
Place filling on the lower third of each tortilla, roll up and place seam-side down in the dish. Repeat with remaining filling and tortillas.
Pour the sauce over the enchiladas, top with cheese, bake covered for 10 minutes, then uncovered for 10 minutes. Serve hot.
Combine the marinade ingredients (yoghurt, lemon juice, turmeric, garam masala, chilli powder, cumin, ginger, garlic) with the chicken and refrigerate for at least 3 hours or overnight.
Heat ghee over high heat in a large frying pan, add the chicken with the marinade clinging to it, and cook for about 3 minutes until white throughout.
Add the tomato passata, cream, sugar, salt and any remaining marinade. Reduce heat to low and simmer for 20 minutes, tasting and adjusting salt as needed.
Garnish with coriander if desired and serve with basmati rice.
Mix the sauce ingredients (cooking wine, oyster sauce, soy sauce, sesame oil, white pepper) in a bowl. Pour 2 teaspoons over the chicken, toss to coat, and set aside 10 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over medium heat, add the egg and scramble until just cooked, then remove.
Turn heat to high, add the remaining oil and bacon, cook 1 minute until the fat melts, then add onion and garlic and cook until the bacon is light golden, about 1.5 minutes.
Add the vegetables (carrot, corn, peas) and cook 1 minute.
Add the chicken and cook until the colour changes from pink to white, about 1.5 minutes.
Add the rice and remaining sauce and cook 1.5 minutes, stirring constantly.
Add the egg and green onion, toss to disperse, and remove from heat.
Preheat oven to 200°C/390°F (180°C fan).
Line a baking dish with foil then baking paper.
Mix the glaze ingredients together (garlic, honey, soy sauce, vinegar, sesame oil, black pepper).
Place the onion and chicken drumsticks in the dish skin side down; pour the glaze over.
Bake for 25 minutes, then turn the chicken.
Bake a further 25-35 minutes until browned and the glaze is sticky.
If the glaze is still thin, remove the chicken and return the pan to the oven to reduce further.
Turn chicken and press into glaze; spoon sauce over to coat.
Serve with rice and greens; sprinkle with sesame seeds and coriander.
Halve the chicken breasts horizontally to create steaks.
Pound the meat between baking paper to about 0.8cm thickness using a rolling pin.
Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Coat in flour (shake off excess), dip in beaten egg (shake off excess), then press into breadcrumbs.
Heat oil in a deep skillet over medium-high heat.
Test the oil temperature by dipping the edge of a schnitzel in - it should sizzle immediately.
Cook 2 schnitzels at a time for 3 minutes per side until deep golden.
Transfer to a paper towel-lined plate; repeat with the remaining pieces.
Serve with lemon wedges.
Sprinkle both sides of chicken with salt, pepper, and Italian herbs; refrigerate 30 minutes.
Heat oil in a saucepan over medium heat; add garlic and onion, cook 3 minutes until translucent.
Add red pepper flakes and herbs; stir 15 seconds, then add wine and simmer until mostly evaporated.
Add tomato passata, chicken stock, salt, and pepper; cover and simmer 10 minutes until thickened. Keep warm.
Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Set up three shallow bowls: flour, whisked egg dredge, and crumbing mixture.
Press chicken into flour (shake off excess), coat in egg (let excess drip off), then press into crumbing mixture.
Heat 1.5-2cm oil in a skillet over medium-high heat to 180°C/350°F.
Carefully place 2-3 chicken pieces in the oil; cook 2 minutes until golden, flip and cook the other side 1.5 minutes.
Transfer to a rack over a paper towel-lined tray; repeat with remaining chicken.
Place chicken on a baking tray and spoon 1/3 cup sauce over each piece, covering about 80% of the surface.
Sprinkle with basil, then mozzarella, then parmesan; drizzle with olive oil.
Bake 15 minutes until the cheese melts with light brown spots, sprinkle with extra basil, and serve immediately.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat.
Add curry paste (and garlic, ginger, lemongrass if using) and cook 2-3 minutes until mostly dry.
Add broth and coconut milk; mix to dissolve the paste.
Add fish sauce, sugar and salt to taste.
Add kaffir lime leaves and bring to a simmer.
Add chicken; cook 7 minutes on medium heat.
Add eggplant; cook 5 minutes until soft.
Taste and adjust seasonings.
Add snow peas; cook 2 minutes.
Stir through basil and lime juice; serve over rice.
Combine marinade ingredients in a bowl; add chicken and coat thoroughly. Marinate 1-24 hours.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat until smoking; char vegetables in batches, seasoning with salt and pepper, then transfer to a platter.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat; cook chicken 3-4 minutes per side until caramelized, rest 3 minutes, then slice.
Warm tortillas and layer with peppers, onion, chicken, sour cream, and a squeeze of lime; fold and serve.
Remove chicken from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking.
Preheat oven to 220°C/450°F (200°C fan).
Mix butter, garlic, sage, rosemary, parsley, salt and pepper; add the juice from 2 lemon wedges.
Loosen the skin over the breast and drumsticks using a spoon.
Push most of the butter under the skin, reserving some for the surface.
Smear remaining butter over the chicken and squeeze over the remaining lemon juice.
Stuff lemon wedges and rosemary inside the cavity.
Tie the drumsticks together with string and tuck the wing tips under the body.
Season the chicken all over with salt and pepper.
Place onion and garlic bulb in the roasting pan, set the chicken on top, pour wine around it and drizzle with oil.
Roast 10 minutes at 220°C then reduce to 180°C/350°F for 1 hour 15 minutes, basting twice, until internal temperature reaches 75°C/165°F.
Rest for 15 minutes uncovered.
Serve with the pan juices.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a dutch oven over medium-high heat; brown chicken skin-side down, flip and brown other side, then transfer to a plate.
Reduce heat, discard excess fat, add onion, garlic, thyme, and oregano; cook 2 minutes.
Add celery and carrots; cook 3 minutes.
Add a splash of broth, scrape the pot bottom, and return the chicken with its juices.
Add the remaining broth and water; bring to a simmer, cover, and cook 30 minutes until the chicken shreds easily.
Remove chicken to a bowl and add the pasta to the pot.
Shred the chicken (discarding skin) and return to the pot; season to taste.
Serve garnished with parsley.
Cut chorizo into quarters vertically, then slice into 0.5cm pieces.
Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a pan; sear chicken until golden but not cooked through, then remove.
Add garlic, onion, and chorizo; cook 1 minute until chorizo browns.
Add capsicum; cook 1 minute.
Add rice; stir until coated with oil.
Add stock, tomato, spinach, half the peas, and paprika; simmer gently.
Place chicken into the pan, mostly submerged.
Cook 10 minutes without stirring, then scatter remaining peas and cook 5 more minutes.
Remove from heat and let stand 5 minutes.
Scatter with parsley and serve.

28.new_title: Thai Chicken Satay with Peanut Sauce (RTE)
method_steps: Combine chicken with marinade ingredients and coconut milk; rest for at least 20 minutes.
Thread 4-5 pieces of chicken onto each skewer.
Heat oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat.
Cook skewers in batches, 3 minutes each side until golden.
Place remaining coconut milk and sauce ingredients in a saucepan over medium-low heat.
Stir and simmer 5 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Adjust consistency with water as needed.
Pour sauce into a serving bowl and sprinkle with peanuts.
Arrange skewers on a platter, garnish with peanuts, coriander, and chilli, and serve with the sauce and jasmine or coconut rice.
Combine the four marinade ingredients in a saucepan over high heat.
Bring to a boil, reduce heat, and simmer until reduced by half to a light syrup consistency (about 5 minutes).
Cool the marinade to room temperature.
Combine cooled marinade with chicken and marinate for at least 20 minutes or overnight.
Remove chicken from marinade, reserving the liquid.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan over medium to medium-high heat and cook the first side about 3 minutes.
Flip and cook the second side, adding 2 tbsp water near the end.
Swirl the pan contents to create a glossy sauce and coat the chicken.
Serve with rice.
Season chicken on both sides with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large pan; brown thighs skin-side down then flip, remove; brown drumsticks similarly.
Reduce heat, sauté onion with rosemary and bay leaves 3 minutes.
Clear the center of the pan, cook garlic and anchovies, mashing the anchovies, then combine with the onion.
Add mushrooms and capsicum; cook until softened, about 5 minutes.
Add tomato paste and cook 2 minutes.
Add wine and reduce by about 75%.
Add stock, canned tomato, salt, pepper, and oregano; bring to a simmer.
Place chicken in the sauce skin-side up, cover, and simmer on medium for 20 minutes.
Remove the lid, add olives, and simmer uncovered 10 minutes to reduce and thicken.
Serve with mashed potato or polenta and parsley.

32.new_title: Sweet and Sour Chicken Stir Fry (RTE)
method_steps: Combine sauce ingredients; mix the cornstarch thickener separately.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat; cook garlic and onion 1 minute.
Add chicken; stir-fry until white outside and still pink inside.
Add peppers; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Pour in the sauce and stir until the sugar dissolves and it starts bubbling.
Add the thickener and simmer, stirring constantly, until syrupy (about 3 minutes).
Stir through the pineapple pieces and remove from heat.
Serve with rice, garnished with shallots.
Mix the seasoning ingredients and coat the chicken with oil and seasoning.
Sear the chicken in a skillet over high heat, 2 minutes per side, then rest and chop.
Sauté onion and garlic 1 minute, then add bell pepper and cook 2 minutes.
Add refried beans, chicken, and water; cook 2 minutes until thickened.
Warm tortillas and layer with rice, filling, corn, cheese, and cilantro.
Roll tightly and pan-fry or bake at 180°C/350°F for 10 minutes.
Mix the marinade ingredients, add the chicken and coat; marinate up to 24 hours if time permits.
Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Place potatoes and onions in a baking pan, top with chicken, and pour over the stock and marinade.
Bake 20 minutes, then remove from the oven.
Scatter with cherry tomatoes, drizzle with olive oil, season, and bake 30-35 minutes until chicken is golden and potatoes are cooked.
Garnish with fresh oregano and serve.

35.new_title: Chicken Stir Fry with Rice Noodles (RTE)
method_steps: Combine chicken, cornstarch, and oil in a bowl.
Whisk together the sauce ingredients.
Soak noodles in hot water per packet directions, then drain.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat.
Add garlic and onion; cook 30 seconds.
Add chicken; cook 1 minute until it changes colour.
Add carrot, choy sum stems, and capsicum; cook 1 minute.
Add mushrooms; cook 30 seconds.
Add noodles, choy sum leaves, and sauce; toss 1 minute until wilted.
Garnish with sesame seeds and green onions; serve immediately.
Combine marinade ingredients (excluding chicken); add chicken and coat thoroughly.
Cover and refrigerate at least 3 hours, preferably overnight.
Heat oil in a non-stick pan over high heat; cook half the chicken 2 minutes per side until charred, then transfer to a bowl.
Discard charred bits, add more oil if needed, and cook the remaining chicken.
Wipe the pan, heat to medium-high, and add oil and butter.
When the butter melts, add onion, ginger, and salt; stir until the ginger turns golden and the onion smells sweet (5-7 minutes).
Reduce heat to medium, add garlic and paprika, and cook 2 minutes.
Add the curry sauce spices and cook 2 minutes, stirring.
Add tomato passata and water, mix, bring to a simmer, cover, and reduce heat to low.
Simmer 15 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Blend the sauce with a stick blender until smooth.
Return sauce to the pan, add cream, sugar, and butter, and stir until melted.
Add chicken and simmer until cooked through.
Optionally sprinkle with extra garam masala before serving.
Serve over basmati rice with a cilantro garnish.
Soak noodles in boiling water for 5 minutes, then drain and rinse with cold water.
Mix the sauce ingredients in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat, add garlic and onion, and cook 30 seconds.
Add chicken and cook 1.5 minutes until mostly cooked through.
Push the chicken to one side, pour the egg on the other side, scramble, then mix together.
Add beansprouts, tofu, noodles, and sauce.
Toss gently for 1.5 minutes until the sauce is absorbed into the noodles.
Add garlic chives and half the peanuts, toss quickly, and remove from heat.
Serve immediately with remaining peanuts, lime wedges, chilli, and extra beansprouts.
Sear the chicken in half the butter until the surface turns white, then remove to a bowl.
Cook the bacon 1 minute, then add leek, celery, and garlic for 5 minutes.
Deglaze the pan with wine and simmer until mostly evaporated.
Melt the remaining butter, sprinkle in the flour, and stir 1 minute.
Slowly add the stock while stirring until the sauce thickens.
Add cream, mustard, herbs, salt, and pepper, then return the chicken; simmer 5 minutes until thick, then cool for 1 hour.
Fill the pie dish with the mixture.
Top with pastry and brush with egg yolk.
Bake 45 minutes at 200°C/375°F until deep golden.

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs, drumsticks or Marylands (bone-in, skin-on)

method_steps: Mix marinade ingredients, add chicken, cover, and marinate 12-24 hours.
Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F; line a tray with foil and place a rack on top.
Remove chicken from marinade and place on the rack; bake 20 minutes.
Generously coat both sides with more marinade; bake 10 minutes.
Brush the top with marinade again; bake 10 minutes.
Give a final baste with marinade and spray with oil, then increase heat to 220°C/450°F and bake 10-15 minutes until charred spots appear.
For the mint yogurt: blend all ingredients until the mint is finely chopped.
Serve the chicken with the mint yogurt.

Combine chicken stock, water, and drumsticks in a medium saucepan over high heat. Simmer for 25 minutes until chicken flesh falls off the bone and liquid reduces by one-third. Remove skin and bones; set broth and shredded chicken aside.
Mix chilli sauce ingredients in a small bowl; set aside for 20 minutes.
Heat oil in a large saucepan over medium-low heat. Saute garlic and ginger for 20 seconds, then add lemongrass and chillies for 1 minute.
Add laksa paste and cook over medium heat for 2 minutes, stirring constantly until fragrant.
Add chicken stock, coconut milk, fish sauce, and 2 tsp of the chilli sauce. Simmer covered for 10 minutes.
Adjust seasoning with lime juice and fish sauce to taste. Add tofu puffs and leave on the turned-off stove, covered, for 5 minutes.
Cook noodles per packet directions. Divide between two bowls, top with shredded chicken and broth. Add bean sprouts and chosen garnishes. Serve chilli sauce on the side.

41.new_title: Caesar Salad (with Homemade Caesar Salad Dressing) (RTE)
method_steps: Blend all dressing ingredients in a food processor until smooth, starting with 3 tbsp milk. Adjust consistency and seasoning. Let sit 20+ minutes.
Cook bacon in a cold skillet over medium-high heat until golden, turning once. Remove to paper towels and chop.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Toast bread briefly until surface dries. Rub with cut garlic, remove crusts, cut into cubes, drizzle with oil, sprinkle salt, and bake 7-15 minutes until golden.
Boil water, add eggs, simmer 3 minutes (soft), 4 minutes (firm), or 6 minutes (hard). Cool in cold water 5 minutes, then peel.
Pound chicken to even thickness or halve horizontally. Season with salt and pepper. Cook 5 minutes per side in bacon fat or oil. Rest 5 minutes, then slice.
Toss lettuce with half the dressing. Transfer to serving bowl, top with chicken and eggs, scatter bacon and croutons, and sprinkle with parmesan.

42.new_title: Baked Honey Mustard Chicken with Potatoes & Bacon (RTE)

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs, skin on bone in
Fresh thyme or parsley, for garnish

method_steps: Cook potatoes in boiling water for 5 minutes; drain. Sprinkle with salt, pepper, and olive oil.
Preheat oven to 190C (375F). Season chicken with salt and pepper.
Heat large skillet over medium-high heat. Add half the bacon; cook until lightly browned. Transfer to paper towels.
Reduce heat to medium. Add chicken skin-side down; cook 3 minutes until lightly browned and fat renders. Flip and cook 2 minutes. Remove to a plate.
Drizzle 1 tbsp rendered fat over potatoes; discard remainder. Return pan to stove.
Add garlic and onion; cook 2 minutes until onion browns and becomes translucent.
Add wine and scrape brown bits from pan bottom. Simmer 2 minutes until alcohol smell dissipates.
Add all remaining sauce ingredients and cooked bacon. Stir to combine.
Return chicken and potatoes to pan; sprinkle remaining bacon on top.
Bake uncovered 40-50 minutes until chicken and potatoes are golden brown.
Rest 5 minutes before serving. Garnish with fresh thyme or parsley if desired.

43.new_title: Sausage Bake with Potatoes and Gravy (RTE)
method_steps: Preheat oven to 200C/390F (standard) or 180C/350F (fan).
Combine vegetable ingredients in a bowl and toss to coat.
Add sausages and toss briefly.
Transfer to a roasting pan with vegetables stacked about 2 deep; arrange sausages on top.
In the same bowl, whisk butter and flour together.
Add broth gradually while whisking.
Pour gravy down the side of the pan, avoiding pouring over the sausages.
Optionally spray sausages with oil.
Bake 25 minutes, then turn sausages and spray again.
Bake another 25 minutes until sausages brown and potatoes soften.
Serve with gravy, garnished with fresh thyme if desired.

44.new_title: Bangers and Mash (Sausage with Onion Gravy) (RTE)
method_steps: Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Add sausages and cook, turning, until browned and cooked through, about 8 minutes.
Remove sausages to a plate. Reduce heat to medium.
Ensure about 2 tbsp oil remains in the skillet; add butter or oil if needed.
Add onion and garlic; cook until golden brown, about 4 minutes.
Add flour and mix through. Cook, stirring constantly, for 2 minutes.
Gradually add beef broth, starting with about 3/4 cup to create a paste, then mix in remaining broth until combined.
Simmer, stirring, for 2-3 minutes until gravy thickens slightly.
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Boil and mash the potatoes with butter and milk; season to taste.
Steam or boil the peas until tender.
Serve sausages with gravy, accompanied by mashed potato and peas.
Cut guanciale into 0.5cm thick slices, then into batons.
Whisk eggs and yolks in a large bowl, then stir in parmesan and pepper.
Bring 4 litres water to boil with salt; add pasta and cook per packet directions.
Reserve 1 cup pasta cooking water before draining the pasta.
Cook guanciale in a non-stick pan over medium-high heat for 4-5 minutes until golden.
Tip the hot pasta into the pan and toss to coat in the guanciale fat.
Transfer pasta and fat into the bowl with the egg mixture; add the reserved pasta cooking water.
Stir vigorously with a wooden spoon handle for 1 minute until the sauce thickens and clings to the pasta.
Serve immediately in warm bowls with optional garnish.
Mix cornflour with 1 tbsp water until lump-free; add remaining sauce ingredients and combine.
Heat oil in a wok/large skillet over high heat. Add garlic and ginger with a quick stir, then add onion.
Cook 1 minute, then add pork mince. Cook until pork turns white.
Add all vegetables; cook 2 minutes until carrot softens and pork cooks through.
Pour in sauce; cook 1.5 minutes until thickened and glossy, coating the filling.
Transfer filling to a serving bowl. Arrange lettuce leaves, peanuts and scallions separately.
To serve: spoon filling into a lettuce leaf, top with peanuts and scallions, bundle and enjoy.

48.new_title: Easy Slow Cooker BBQ Pulled Pork (RTE)

ingredients.name: Beer, apple cider, or apple juice

method_steps: Mix rub ingredients together; coat pork thoroughly.
Place in slow cooker, pour liquid over meat.
Slow cook on low for 10 hours until the meat shreds easily.
Transfer to a roasting pan; roast at 180C (350F) for 20 minutes.
Remove the fat layer; roast another 15 minutes.
Shred the pork using two forks.
Combine sauce ingredients in a saucepan; simmer 45 minutes until thickened.
Add 3/4-1 cup of the slow cooker juices to the sauce.
Toss the pork gently with the BBQ sauce.
Serve on sliders or dinner plates with sides.

49.new_title: Sweet and Sour Pork - best ever! (RTE)

ingredients.name: Pork scotch roast or shoulder, cubed
Vegetable or canola oil, for frying

method_steps: Mix pork with marinade ingredients and marinate 18-24 hours.
Preheat oven to 80C/175F with a rack on a tray.
Combine all sauce ingredients except water in a jug; mix in water.
Mix cornflour into marinated pork; let sit 5 minutes until sticky.
Spread cornflour in a shallow bowl and coat pork pieces, shaking off excess.
Heat oil to 180C/350F in a large saucepan.
Fry pork in batches for 3 minutes until golden (Fry #1); drain on a rack.
Increase oil temperature to 200C/390F.
Fry pork again for 1.5 minutes until deep golden brown (Fry #2); transfer to the warm oven.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Stir-fry garlic, ginger, and onion for 1.5 minutes.
Add capsicum and stir for 2 minutes.
Add sauce and pineapple pieces; simmer 2 minutes until thickened.
Add fried pork and toss quickly for about 10 seconds.
Serve immediately.

50.new_title: Pork Stir Fry with Green Beans (RTE)
method_steps: Combine sauce ingredients in a bowl.
Trim bean stems and chop into 2-2.5cm pieces.
Heat 1.5 tbsp oil in a cast iron skillet over high heat until smoking. Add beans spread flat for 1 minute, stir and cook 30 seconds, repeat until charred (2.5 minutes total). Transfer to a bowl.
Reduce heat to medium-high, add 1 tbsp oil. Cook onion until golden edges appear, then add garlic and ginger for 1 minute.
Increase heat to high, add pork, breaking it apart while cooking for 2 minutes until done. Pour in sauce, cook 30 seconds.
Add charred beans, stir for another 30 seconds.
Serve over rice.
Pound pork between baking paper to 0.5cm thickness; season both sides with salt and pepper.
Set up three bowls: flour, whisked eggs, and panko mixed with Herbes de Provence.
Coat each cutlet in flour, shake off excess, dip in egg, then press into the breadcrumb mixture on both sides.
Heat oil in a frying pan over medium-high heat; cook two schnitzels at a time for 2 minutes per side until golden.
Transfer to an oven rack at 70C while cooking the remaining schnitzels.
Rest 2 minutes, finish with lemon juice and salt flakes.
Bring a large pot of water to the boil. Cook pasta per packet directions minus 1-2 minutes. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Cut sausages into 1.5cm chunks using a sharp knife. Pieces will be irregular; this corrects during cooking.
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat. Add sausages; cook until browned outside (doesn't need to be cooked through).
Add onion and garlic; cook 2 minutes until translucent and sausages very browned.
Add tomato, chilli flakes, salt, and pepper. Reduce heat to medium; simmer energetically for 8 minutes until thickened, stirring occasionally.
Add spinach (if using), pasta, and about 1/2 cup pasta water. Toss for no longer than 1 minute to avoid breaking the pasta.
Remove from heat; serve immediately garnished with basil and parmesan.
Heat oil in a large skillet; saute onion and garlic 1 minute, then add carrots, celery, thyme and rosemary for 3 minutes until softened.
Increase heat to high; add lamb, breaking it up while browning.
Mix in flour, tomato paste, broth, red wine, bouillon cube, Worcestershire sauce and bay leaves.
Simmer 30 minutes on medium heat, stirring occasionally, until the sauce thickens to gravy consistency.
Add salt and pepper; taste and adjust. Transfer to a baking dish, stir through peas, cool 1-2 hours or overnight.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F.
Boil potatoes in salted water 15 minutes until soft; drain and steam dry 30 seconds.
Mash potatoes with butter, then add milk, salt and pepper until creamy and spreadable.
Spread mash over the filling; create fork patterns on the surface.
Sprinkle parmesan and drizzle butter over the top.
Bake 30-40 minutes until deep golden and bubbling at the edges; verify the centre is piping hot with a knife test.
Stand 5 minutes before serving; garnish with fresh thyme if desired.
Preheat oven to 170C/335F (150C fan-forced).
Place garlic, onion and rosemary in a metal roasting pan.
Season lamb leg with salt and pepper on both sides.
Place lamb upside down on the aromatics, drizzle with olive oil.
Pour broth and water around the lamb. Cover with foil.
Roast for 4.5 hours until the meat is pull-apart tender.
Turn the lamb over and roast uncovered for 20-30 minutes until browned.
Rest for 15 minutes on a platter loosely covered with foil.
For gravy: skim fat from pan juices, heat on the stovetop, scatter in flour and whisk in.
Whisk in water until gravy consistency is reached.
Strain into a bowl, pressing flavour from the aromatics.
Pour into a gravy jug and serve.

ingredients.name: Shredded red cabbage, carrot and mint salad

method_steps: Combine yoghurt sauce ingredients; refrigerate a minimum of 20 minutes.
Grate onion into a bowl; add remaining kofta ingredients and mix thoroughly with hands.
With damp hands, divide meat into 8 equal portions and mould onto skewers, creating smooth or textured surfaces.
Stove: heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat; cook 5 minutes total, turning to brown evenly.
BBQ: cook on medium-high for about 6 minutes, rotating until browned.
Broiler: preheat; cook 30cm from the heat source for 12 minutes, rotating as needed.
Assemble wraps by spreading yoghurt sauce on flatbread, topping with salad and kofta; drizzle with additional sauce.
Melt ghee over medium heat; add cinnamon, cardamom, and cloves for one minute.
Add onion and cook 7 minutes until golden and browning at the edges. Stir in garlic and ginger; cook 1 minute more.
Add all ground spices; cook 30 seconds.
Mix in tomato passata and salt, then add stock. Add lamb and stir; bring to a simmer.
Cover and simmer on low-medium heat for 1 hour 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is fork-tender.
Remove the lid and cook 15 minutes more to reduce the sauce; the lamb should be very tender.
Stir in yoghurt, extra garam masala, and fennel; cook several minutes longer.
Serve with basmati rice and garnishes.

59.new_title: Lamb Chops with Rosemary Gravy (loin chops, forequarter, cutlets) (RTE)
method_steps: Mix marinade ingredients and coat chops; marinate a minimum of 1 hour, preferably 3 hours or overnight.
Remove lamb from the refrigerator 30 minutes before cooking.
Heat a large skillet over high heat without oil.
Scrape excess garlic from the chops; place in the skillet. Reduce heat to medium-high.
Cook loin and forequarter chops 4 minutes per side; cutlets and leg steaks 3 minutes first side, 2 minutes second side, for medium-rare at 62C/145F internal temperature.
Remove lamb to a plate; loosely cover with foil and rest 3 minutes.
Pour off excess oil, leaving 3 tbsp (add butter if needed).
Stir in flour for 30 seconds over medium heat.
Gradually pour in beef stock while stirring to dissolve the flour mixture.
Add chopped rosemary and a sprig; simmer 2-3 minutes, stirring regularly, until gravy consistency is reached.
Boil and mash the potatoes with butter and milk; season to taste.
Serve the lamb chops with rosemary gravy and mashed potato.
Layer eggplant in a colander, sprinkle with salt, and rest 30 minutes while preparing sauces.
Preheat oven to 240°C (450°F). Pat eggplant dry, brush with oil, and bake 15-20 minutes until softened.
Heat oil, sauté onion and garlic 2 minutes. Add lamb mince, brown, breaking apart.
Add wine, cook 1.5 minutes. Stir in remaining filling ingredients, simmer 15 minutes until thickened.
Melt butter, add flour, cook 1 minute. Gradually add milk, stirring until thickened (3-5 minutes).
Remove from heat, whisk in cheese, nutmeg, stock powder, pepper. Cool 5 minutes, whisk in eggs.
Lower oven to 180°C (350°F). Layer half the eggplant in a baking dish, top with filling, then remaining eggplant.
Pour béchamel over, sprinkle breadcrumbs. Bake 30-40 minutes until golden brown.
Rest 10 minutes before serving.

ingredients.name: Greek salad / shredded lettuce and tomato

method_steps: Combine marinade ingredients in a bowl; add lamb and coat thoroughly.
Refrigerate 12-24 hours minimum.
Thread 3-4 pieces onto each skewer.
Sprinkle meat with salt and pepper.
Heat olive oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat.
Cook half the skewers 3 minutes per side until seared (target 60°C/140°F internal temperature).
Transfer to a plate and cover loosely with foil while cooking remaining skewers.
Serve in pita wraps with tzatziki and salad, or on plates with rice and vegetables.

62.new_title: Lemon Garlic Salmon Tray Bake - Easy & Healthy! (RTE)
method_steps: Combine marinade ingredients; slather onto salmon's top and sides. Marinate 1 hour if time permits, otherwise proceed immediately.
Preheat oven grill/broiler to 280°C/525°F (or maximum setting). Position shelf 20cm/8" from heat source.
Toss asparagus and tomatoes with olive oil, salt, and pepper. Arrange on tray, clearing space for salmon. Place fillets with spacing between each; spray surfaces with oil.
Broil for 11 minutes until salmon flakes and reaches 50°C/122°F internal temperature.
Transfer to plates; grate Parmesan over vegetables. Squeeze lemon over salmon; garnish with parsley. Serve immediately.

ingredients.name: Chinese black vinegar or rice vinegar

method_steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F (160°C fan). Line a tray with parchment paper; place shelf in middle.
Mix water and cornflour in a small bowl to create a slurry.

Place all remaining glaze ingredients in a small saucepan over medium-low heat. Simmer 2 minutes. Add cornflour slurry and stir well. Cook 2 more minutes until thickened to a thick paste consistency (should stick to fish, not slide off).
Place fish fillets on prepared tray. Dab and brush glaze onto top and sides of each fillet.
Bake for 15 minutes. Switch to broiler/grill on high for 3 minutes to caramelise the surface. Target internal temperature: 55°C/131°F (medium doneness).
Remove from oven and transfer to serving plates. Rest 5 minutes. Garnish with green onion, chillies, and sesame seeds. Serve with cucumber salad tossed in Asian sesame dressing.
Combine marinade ingredients in a ziplock bag and marinate fish for 20 minutes (no longer than 1 hour).
Place pickled cabbage ingredients in a bowl, toss, and set aside for 30 minutes. Drain excess liquid and scrunch cabbage to soften.
Mix sour cream and sriracha together.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook fish 2 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Remove fish and flake into large pieces.
Assemble tacos with cabbage, fish, a dollop of sour cream sauce, and a squeeze of lime. Fold tortilla and serve.
Cook egg noodles according to packet instructions; drain and set aside.
Steam broccolini until crisp-tender (2-3 minutes); set aside to drain.
Mix all sauce ingredients except water in a bowl until cornflour dissolves, then stir in water.
Heat oil in a wok on medium-high. Add prawns in a single layer; cook 1½ minutes per side until cooked through. Remove to a bowl.
Add onion; cook 1 minute. Add ginger and garlic; cook 1 minute until softening.
Add snow peas; toss until coated. Add broccolini; toss through.
Stir sauce and pour into pan. Cook, stirring, until thickened (about 1 minute).
Return prawns with juices and add cooked noodles; toss to combine and warm through. Serve immediately.
Boil salted water and cook pasta one minute under package time. Reserve 1 cup pasta water before draining.
Melt one-third of the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat. Cook half the prawns 1½ minutes per side until barely cooked. Transfer to bowl. Repeat with remaining butter and prawns.
Add remaining butter to skillet. Stir garlic for 20 seconds until fragrant.
Add wine and simmer 2 minutes, scraping pan until mostly evaporated.
Add cream, broth, and parmesan. Stir until melted; simmer 1½ minutes.
Add prawns, pasta, and ¼ cup reserved pasta water. Toss energetically on medium-high heat 1-2 minutes until sauce thickens and coats pasta. Add more pasta water if needed.
Stir in parsley and black pepper. Taste for seasoning and serve immediately with extra parmesan.
Make curry paste: combine all curry paste ingredients in a tall jug or blender container. Blend on high for 5-10 seconds until onion is pureed, adding more water if needed.
Heat oil over medium heat in a large pot. Add black mustard seeds and let sizzle for 30 seconds.
Add sliced red onion and cook for 3 minutes until edges turn golden.
Add curry paste and cook for 3 minutes to evaporate water and bloom spices.
Increase heat to medium-high. Add tomato paste and tomato pulp, cook for 2 minutes.
Add water, coconut milk, sugar, salt, and chilli powder. Stir and bring to a gentle simmer on low heat.
Simmer for 2 minutes, then add tomato chunks and green chillies. Simmer another 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until sauce thickens.
Add fish cubes, stir gently, and cook for 3-4 minutes until fish flakes easily.
Transfer to a serving bowl. Garnish with coriander and fresh green chillies. Serve with basmati rice.

71.new_title: Creamy Salmon Risoni / Orzo (15 Minute Meal) (RTE)
method_steps: Melt butter in a deep skillet over medium-high heat.
Add onion and garlic; cook 2 minutes until translucent.
Add flour; cook 1 minute.
Whisk in half the milk to dissolve mixture, then add remaining milk, broth, risoni, and peas.
Simmer gently for 5 minutes, stirring occasionally then more frequently.
Remove from heat while still saucy.
Stir in parmesan, salt, and pepper to taste.
Gently fold in salmon.
Serve immediately with parsley.

72.new_title: Chinese Fried Rice with Shrimp / Prawns (RTE)

ingredients.name: Cooked long grain white rice, chilled

method_steps: Mix sauce ingredients together; set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a wok over medium heat. Scramble eggs, remove to a plate.
Increase heat to high, add bacon. Cook until golden (about 2 minutes), remove to a plate.
Drain excess bacon fat.
Add remaining oil with garlic and ginger on high heat. Stir fry as it heats without burning. When sizzling, add onion; stir fry 2 minutes until golden.
Add Chinese wine and sugar; simmer rapidly for 20 seconds until mostly evaporated.
Add prawns; stir fry 1 minute to heat through.
Add rice, sauce, eggs, bacon, and most scallions. Stir fry 2 minutes until rice is hot.
Transfer to serving plate, garnish with remaining scallions, serve.
Insert cloves into onion quarters. Add to a pot with milk; heat to a gentle simmer over medium-high.
Poach fish for 7 minutes; transfer to a baking dish using a slotted spoon. Flake gently; remove bones if visible.
Remove onion and cloves from milk; discard. Reserve milk. Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Melt butter in a large skillet; sauté onion and garlic 1 minute. Add celery and carrot; cook 5 minutes until soft.
Reduce heat to medium-low; add flour and mix until paste-like forms.
Add half the reserved milk, stirring quickly. Add remaining milk once incorporated. The mixture thickens rapidly.
Increase heat to medium; stir constantly until consistency resembles honey, approximately 3 minutes.
Remove from heat; stir through tarragon and parsley. Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Scatter peas over flaked fish; gently pour sauce over without breaking fish apart.
Smooth surface; nestle egg quarters throughout.
Meanwhile, boil potatoes until soft (15 minutes). Drain; add butter, milk, salt, and pepper. Mash until creamy.
Immediately spread mash onto pie; create fork-drawn texture patterns across surface.
Drizzle melted butter over top; sprinkle with parmesan.
Bake 35 minutes until deep golden. Stand 5 minutes before serving; garnish with parsley if desired.

74.new_title: Lentil Curry - Mega Flavour Lentil Recipe! (RTE)
method_steps: Melt butter in a pot over medium heat; add onion, garlic, ginger. Cook 10 minutes, stirring occasionally, until golden.
Increase heat to high; add curry powder and turmeric. Stir for 1½ minutes.
Add remaining ingredients; stir and bring to a simmer. Cover and reduce heat to low-medium.
Simmer 30 minutes covered, then 10 minutes uncovered to thicken sauce.
Lentils should be soft; adjust consistency with water if needed. Stir in half the fresh coriander; taste and adjust salt.
Serve over basmati rice, topped with coriander and yoghurt.
Pat tofu dry, cut into 2cm cubes, and pan-fry in a little oil until golden on all sides; set aside.
Slice carrot diagonally 2mm thick; separate bok choy stems and leaves.
Mix cornflour with soy sauce until dissolved; combine with remaining sauce ingredients.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat; stir-fry garlic and ginger for 10 seconds.
Add onion; stir 30 seconds.
Add carrot, capsicum, and bok choy stems; stir 1 minute.
Add mushrooms; stir 2-3 minutes until nearly cooked.
Pour sauce in; toss 1 minute until glossy and thickened. Return tofu to the wok and toss to combine.
Serve over rice or noodles; garnish with green onions and sesame seeds.
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat; add garlic and onion, cook until translucent (about 2 minutes).
Add carrot, celery, zucchini; stir for 1 minute.
Add crushed tomato, vegetable stock, water, tomato paste, vegetarian Worcestershire sauce, kidney beans, salt, and pepper.
Bring to a simmer, cover, and adjust heat to a gentle simmer.
Simmer 20 minutes; add potato and green beans.
Simmer 5 minutes; add pasta (cook for packet time minus 1.5 minutes).
Remove from heat; stir in extra parmesan and spinach.
Taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve garnished with extra parmesan and parsley.
Heat oil in a wok/skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add onion and garlic; cook 1 minute until fragrant.
Add vegetables (frozen acceptable); cook 2 minutes to defrost.
Add rice and sauce ingredients; toss 1½ minutes until liquid evaporates.
Push rice aside; add oil to cleared space and scramble eggs thoroughly.
Stir in green onions; combine with rice and serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 200°C (390°F). Toss pumpkin, zucchini and onion wedges with olive oil, garlic, salt and pepper on a baking tray.
Roast vegetables for 25 minutes, turning at the 15 minute mark, until tender and browned. Loosen from tray with a spatula while still warm.
Heat olive oil in a skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and onion and cook for 3 minutes until golden.
Add passata, crushed tomato, water, dried thyme, oregano, chilli flakes, salt and pepper. Stir to combine.
Simmer sauce on medium-low for 20 minutes, then adjust salt to taste (add a touch of sugar if too sour). Cool 5 minutes.
Mix ricotta, spinach, parmesan, egg, garlic, nutmeg, salt and pepper together in a bowl to make the ricotta filling.
Preheat oven to 160°C (320°F). Spread a ladle of sauce over the base of a large baking dish.
Cover with lasagne sheets, tearing to fit. Top with roasted vegetables and roasted red pepper strips.
Spoon over 2½ cups of sauce and sprinkle with half the mozzarella. Cover with another layer of lasagne sheets and spread with the ricotta mixture.
Cover with a final layer of lasagne sheets, pour over remaining sauce and sprinkle with remaining mozzarella.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until cheese is golden. Rest 5 minutes before serving.
Bring salted water to the boil, add macaroni and cook for 1 minute less than packet directions, then drain.
Toss the drained pasta with 1 tablespoon of butter and set aside to cool slightly.
Combine panko breadcrumbs, melted butter and salt for the topping; set aside.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Melt butter in a large saucepan over medium heat, add flour and stir constantly for 1 minute to make a roux.
Whisk in 1 cup of milk until smooth, then gradually whisk in remaining milk until lump-free.
Stir in salt, garlic powder, onion powder and mustard powder.
Cook sauce for 5-8 minutes, stirring regularly, until it coats the back of a spoon.
Remove from heat and stir in the Gruyere and mozzarella until melted and smooth.
Pour cheese sauce over the pasta and stir to combine; transfer to a baking dish.
Sprinkle breadcrumb topping over the pasta.
Bake for 25 minutes until golden on top. Serve immediately.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Heat olive oil in a cast iron skillet over medium-high heat. Add garlic and onion and cook for 2 minutes until translucent.
Add capsicum and cook for 1 minute, then add diced tomato and cook 2 minutes until it starts to break down.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, stock, paprika, cumin, salt and pepper. Mix well.
Reduce heat to medium-low and simmer for 5 minutes until thickened but still saucy.
Make indentations in the sauce and crack the eggs into them. Cook for 1 minute until the egg white edges start to set.
Transfer the skillet to the oven and bake for 7-12 minutes until the whites are set but yolks are still runny.
Remove from the oven, garnish with herbs and serve immediately with crusty bread or pita.
Preheat oven to 220°C (430°F). Toss pumpkin, zucchini and capsicum with olive oil, garlic, dried herbs, salt and pepper on a baking tray.
Roast for 25 minutes without turning.
Reduce oven to 180°C (350°F). Grease and line a 22cm square (or 19x30cm) pan with baking paper.
Whisk eggs, cream, salt and pepper together in a bowl.
Spread two-thirds of the roasted vegetables over the base of the pan. Pour over the egg mixture, sprinkle with cheese, then top with the remaining vegetables.
Scatter over the crumbled feta and sliced mushroom, drizzling the mushroom lightly with olive oil.
Bake for 40 minutes until the centre is set. Rest 5 minutes before slicing and serving.
Preheat oven to 180°C (350°F).
Melt 1 tablespoon of butter in an ovenproof pot over medium-high heat.
Add garlic and onion and cook until the onion is translucent.
Add chopped sage and cook for 1 minute.
Add the rice and stir until the grains are coated and semi-translucent.
Add white wine and cook until the liquid has evaporated, about 1 minute.
Add pumpkin and stock, stir, and bring just to a simmer without boiling.
Cover with a lid or foil and bake for 25 minutes until the rice is just cooked.
Stir in the remaining butter and parmesan vigorously until the pumpkin mashes into a creamy sauce. Add a splash of hot water if too thick.
For the crispy sage, melt butter in a pan over medium-high heat, add sage leaves and stir gently for 1.5 minutes until crispy. Drain on paper towel.
Spoon risotto into bowls and garnish with crispy sage, brown butter and parmesan.
Soak dried chickpeas in cold water overnight (12+ hours).
Drain the chickpeas well and add to a food processor with parsley, coriander, spring onion, garlic, cumin, coriander, salt and baking powder.
Blitz for 2-3 minutes on high, scraping down the sides, until the mixture resembles fine grains. Add the flour and water and pulse to combine.
Scoop heaped tablespoons of the mixture and roll into balls, about 2.5cm in diameter (makes about 20). Refrigerate for 30 minutes.
Heat oil to 180-190°C in a skillet or pot (at least 1.7cm deep).
Fry the falafel in batches for about 4 minutes, rolling with forks, until deep golden and crispy. Drain on paper towel.
Mix tahini and lemon juice, then stir in water a tablespoon at a time until drizzling consistency. Season with salt.
Stuff pita or flatbread with falafel, tomato, onion and lettuce, and drizzle with tahini sauce to serve as wraps.
Mix flour, yeast, sugar and salt in a large bowl. Make a well, add oil and warm water and mix until the dough leaves the bowl clean. Knead for 5 minutes until smooth, then shape into a ball.
Place dough in an oiled bowl, cover, and let rise for 1-2 hours until doubled (or refrigerate up to 5 days).
Preheat oven to 275°C (530°F). Stretch the dough ball out to about 25-28cm and place on a pizza pan.
Spread pizza sauce evenly over the base.
Sprinkle over the mozzarella cheese and arrange the pepperoni slices on top.
Bake for 10 minutes, rotating at the 4-minute mark, until the crust is golden and the cheese is bubbling. Check at 8 minutes and remove earlier if the cheese browns too much.
Boil salted water and cook the fettuccine for 2 minutes less than packet directions. Reserve 250ml of pasta water, then drain.
Heat a non-stick pan and cook the bacon until golden. Remove and drain on paper towel, reserving the bacon fat in the pan.
Season the chicken with salt and pepper and cook in the bacon fat for 2 minutes per side. Remove, rest, then slice.
Melt the butter in the pan over medium heat and sauté the garlic and onion for 3-4 minutes until soft.
Add the cream, reserved pasta water, parmesan, salt and pepper. Simmer for 2 minutes.
Add the pasta and toss for about 1.5 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the noodles.
Mix in the bacon and chicken, taste and adjust seasoning.
Serve immediately with extra parmesan and parsley.
Combine chicken with cumin, garlic powder, cayenne, paprika, salt and pepper in a bowl.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook onion and garlic for 1 minute, then add capsicum and cook 2 minutes until softening. Transfer to a bowl.
Return the skillet to the heat and cook the chicken until browned. Combine with the vegetable mixture and cool completely.
Preheat oven to 220°C (430°F) standard or 200°C (390°F) fan-forced. Spray a baking tray with oil and heat it in the oven.
Divide the filling between the tortillas on one half, top with cheese, fold in half and press gently.
Carefully transfer the quesadillas to the hot tray.
Bake for 8 minutes, then flip and press with a spatula. Bake for another 8 minutes until crispy.
Serve immediately.
Heat oil in a wok or skillet over high heat until smoking.
Add onion and garlic and cook for 1.5 minutes until fragrant.
Add the peas (and other vegetables) and cook for 2 minutes to defrost and evaporate excess water.
Add the rice and sauce ingredients and cook for 1.5 minutes, stirring, until the liquid has evaporated.
Push the rice to the side, add a little oil to the cleared space and scramble the eggs thoroughly.
Stir the egg through the rice along with the green onions and serve immediately.

98.new_title: Quick Asian Caramelised Beef Ramen Noodles (RTE)
method_steps: Mix the dark soy sauce, oyster sauce, hoisin sauce and mirin together to make the sauce.
Heat the oil and sesame oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook the onion and garlic for 1.5 minutes until golden.
Add the beef mince, breaking it up, and cook until browned.
Add the sauce and cook for 2-3 minutes until caramelized.
Push the beef aside, add the water and the uncooked noodles.
After 45 seconds, turn the noodles; after another 30 seconds, untangle and toss through the beef.
Add the bean sprouts and toss for 1 minute until the sauce coats the noodles.
Serve garnished with green onion and sesame seeds.
Preheat oven to 220°C (200°C fan-forced).
Toss tomatoes and unpeeled garlic with oil, paprika, salt and pepper; arrange cut-side up on a tray.
Roast for 40-45 minutes until charred.
Sauté minced garlic and onion in oil for 3 minutes until translucent.
Add roasted tomatoes, juices, squeezed garlic flesh, and stock.
Blitz with a stick blender until smooth.
Simmer 10 minutes, then stir in cream.
Ladle into bowls; drizzle with cream and garnish with basil.
Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan).
Season beef with half the salt and pepper.
Heat oil and butter; cook onion 6 minutes until golden.
Add beef, stir until surfaces brown (about 2 minutes).
Add garlic, capsicum, tomato; stir 3 minutes.
Add paprika, caraway and bay leaf; stir 30 seconds.
Add stock; bring to a simmer.
Cover and bake 1.5 hours.
Stir in carrots and potatoes; cook covered 30 minutes.
Ladle into bowls; garnish with parsley.
Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Use ramekins as guides to cut 4 pastry rounds from the puff pastry sheet.
Heat oil in a large pan over medium-high heat.
Sauté onion and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add carrots and cook 1 minute, then add mushrooms for another minute.
Combine remaining filling ingredients (beef, sauce, peas, tomato, tomato paste, water, herbs, salt, pepper); simmer 3 minutes.
Divide filling among 4 ramekins; top each with a pastry round and cut steam vents.
Brush pastry with beaten egg.
Bake 20-25 minutes until pastry is golden and puffed.
Rest 5 minutes before serving.
Cut beef into 3.25cm cubes.
Mix marinade ingredients; add beef and marinate 3-24 hours.
Cut capsicum and onion into 3.25cm squares.
Thread beef and vegetables onto skewers loosely (2 vegetable pieces between each meat piece).
Brush with reserved marinade and drizzle with olive oil.
Cook on high heat 2 minutes per side (4 sides total), basting with marinade.
Rest 3 minutes before serving; garnish with parsley.
Char onion and ginger cut-side down in a hot skillet, turn and remove.
Lightly toast the spices in a dry skillet over medium-high heat for 3 minutes.
Rinse bones and brisket, cover with water in a large pot.
Boil for 5 minutes, then drain completely and rinse bones and brisket under the tap.
Wipe pot clean and bring 3.5 litres fresh water to the boil.
Add bones, brisket, charred onion, ginger, toasted spices, sugar and salt; cover and simmer 3 hours.
Remove brisket, cool, and refrigerate for later use.
Simmer remaining broth uncovered for a further 40 minutes.
Strain broth into another pot, discarding bones and spices (yield about 2.5 litres).
Add fish sauce and adjust salt and sugar to taste.
Prepare rice noodles per packet instructions just before serving.
Place noodles in a bowl, top with raw beef and brisket slices.
Ladle about 400ml hot broth over the bowl contents (this cooks the raw beef).
Serve with toppings on the side for self-service.
Blend spice paste ingredients in a food processor until fine.
Heat oil in a large pot over high heat; brown half the beef, remove; repeat with remaining beef.
Lower heat to medium-low; add spice paste, cook 2-3 minutes until darkened.
Add remaining curry ingredients and beef; stir to combine.
Bring to a simmer, reduce heat to low, cover with lid.
Simmer 1 hour 15 minutes.
Remove lid; check beef tenderness (should be quite tender).
Increase heat to medium; reduce sauce 30-40 minutes, stirring frequently until beef browns and sauce becomes paste-like.
Remove from heat and serve with rice.
Preheat oven to 180°C. Oil a loaf tin.
Mix glaze ingredients; set aside.
Combine breadcrumbs and grated onion in a large bowl until wet.
Add remaining meatloaf ingredients; mix thoroughly by hand.
Form into loaf shape, smoothing creases.
Transfer to tin; brush with half the glaze.
Bake 45 minutes; brush with remaining glaze and remove excess fat.
Bake additional 30 minutes.
Rest 10 minutes before slicing.
Marinate beef 24 hours in wine with onion, carrot, thyme and bay leaf.
Strain and reserve wine.
Reduce wine by half (about 7 minutes on medium-high heat).
Pat beef dry and season with salt and pepper.
Brown beef aggressively in batches; set aside.
Cook bacon until golden; remove.
Sauté mushrooms until golden in a separate pan.
Sauté onions until golden patches appear.
Add butter, then sauté carrots until golden.
Add garlic, cook 1 minute.
Add tomato paste, cook 2 minutes.
Add flour, cook 2 minutes.
Slowly pour in beef stock while stirring; add wine.
Add beef, bacon, thyme, bay leaf, salt and pepper.
Cover and bake at 180°C/350°F for 1 hour.
Stir in mushrooms and onions.
Bake covered for 1.5 hours until fork-tender.
Adjust thickness and salt to taste; garnish with parsley.
Grate onion over breadcrumbs; let soak briefly.
Combine all steak ingredients until mixture becomes pasty.
Form 5 oval patties approximately 1.7cm thick.
Heat oil in skillet over high heat; brown patties one minute per side, then remove.
Sauté chopped onion and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add mushrooms; cook 2-3 minutes until golden.
Reduce heat to medium; add butter then flour; stir 30 seconds.
Gradually add broth while stirring; whisk in remaining gravy ingredients.
Return patties to skillet; cook 5-7 minutes until gravy thickens.
Remove steaks; season gravy to taste; serve over steaks.
Create curry paste by blending spices with ginger, garlic and water.
Marinate beef in the paste for 2+ hours.
Prepare sauce base: sauté onion, ginger, garlic; add mustard seeds, curry leaves and tomato paste.
Puree sauce with a stick blender.
Add beef and marinade along with beef stock; bring to a simmer.
Slow-cook in the oven (190°C) for 2 hours until tender.
Garnish with fresh coriander; serve with rice and naan.
Mix mayonnaise and garlic; set aside 30+ minutes for flavour development.

Melt butter over medium-low heat; add onions and stir, cover for 20 minutes, stirring occasionally. Remove lid and cook 20 more minutes until golden. Add sugar, vinegar, salt and pepper; cook 10 minutes until jammy.
Remove beef from fridge 20 minutes before cooking. Season generously.
Heat oil in a skillet over high heat. Cook steak to desired doneness (medium-rare recommended for secondary cuts).
Transfer to a plate, cover loosely with foil, rest 5-10 minutes. Slice thinly against the grain.
Cut bread into 15cm lengths and split. Toast lightly.
Spread bottom piece generously with garlic aioli. Layer rocket, tomato, beef and caramelised onion. Spread top piece with mustard and place on top.
Heat oil; sauté onion, celery, carrot and garlic for 10 minutes until softened.
Add beef, brown thoroughly.
Add remaining ragu ingredients except sugar; simmer covered 1.5-2 hours, then 30 minutes uncovered.
Warm milk; melt butter, add flour, cook 1 minute.
Gradually incorporate milk; whisk until smooth and thickens (5-8 minutes).
Remove from heat; stir in cheese, nutmeg, salt and pepper.
Preheat oven to 180°C; smear ragu on the base of a baking dish.
Layer pasta sheets, ragu, and cheese sauce; repeat twice more.
Top with a final pasta layer, remaining cheese sauce, and mozzarella.
Bake 25 minutes until golden and bubbling.
Stand 5-10 minutes before serving.
Mix beef with sauce; marinate 20 minutes.
Prepare noodles per packet directions; drain and rinse.
Heat oil; cook beef 1.5 minutes until done; transfer to bowl.
Heat oil; add garlic and onion (30 seconds); add capsicum (1 minute); transfer to bowl.
Add sauce, water and chilli paste; cook 30 seconds until thickening; add scallions and snow peas (30 seconds).
Return beef mixture to wok; add noodles; stir fry 1.5 minutes.
Serve immediately with garnishes.

ingredients.name: Chicken breast or thigh fillets, sliced

method_steps: Combine cornstarch and soy sauce, then mix in remaining sauce ingredients.
Coat chicken with 1 tbsp sauce; marinate 10 minutes.
Prepare noodles per packet instructions; drain.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat.
Stir-fry garlic 10 seconds until golden.
Add chicken; cook about 1 minute until surface browns.
Add cabbage, carrot and white onion pieces; stir-fry 1.5 minutes.
Add noodles, sauce and water; toss constantly for 1 minute.
Add bean sprouts and remaining onions; toss 30 seconds.
Serve immediately.

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs, skin on, bone in, halved

method_steps: Mix all marinade ingredients in a large pot; add chicken and coat thoroughly. Marinate 20 minutes to overnight.
Bring 3 litres water to the boil; add salt and whole spices (cloves, bay leaves, star anise, cardamom pods).
Add rice, return to boil, then cook 4 minutes until just cooked but still firm in the centre. Drain immediately.
Heat 1 cup oil in a large saucepan over medium-high heat. Cook onion in batches 3-4 minutes until golden brown; drain on paper towel.
Place saffron threads in a bowl with warm water; steep 10+ minutes.
Place pot with marinated chicken on the stove over medium heat. Cover and cook 5 minutes.
Remove lid. Cook 5 minutes, turning chicken twice.
Remove from heat. Turn chicken skin-side down to cover most of the pot base.
Scatter half the fried onions over the chicken, then half the coriander.
Top with all the par-boiled rice. Gently pat down and flatten the surface.
Drizzle saffron water in a random pattern across the rice surface. Drizzle melted ghee over the top.
Cover the pot with a lid. Return to the stove over medium heat. Once steam appears, reduce to low and cook 25 minutes.
Remove from the stove. Keep the lid on and rest 10 minutes.
Scoop layers of yellow rice, white rice, curry-stained rice and chicken together onto a platter. Garnish with remaining fried onions and coriander. Serve with yoghurt on the side.

117.new_title: Katsu Curry (Japanese Curry with Chicken Cutlet) (RTE)
method_steps: Heat oil in pot over medium-high heat; sauté onion until translucent with browned edges.
Add potatoes and carrots; stir 2 minutes until surfaces begin cooking.
Add water, bring to a boil, reduce heat to medium-low, simmer 7 minutes until vegetables are nearly tender.
Break curry roux into blocks, add to the pot, and stir gently to blend.
Reduce heat to low, cover, cook 10 minutes until roux dissolves, stirring occasionally.
Check sauce consistency (should resemble béchamel); adjust with water if needed.
Turn off the heat.
Place rice on a plate, lean cutlet pieces against the rice, pour curry beside the cutlet, and add fukujinzuke.
Pound chicken breasts to 1cm thickness between plastic wrap using a meat mallet.
Season with salt and pepper, then dust with flour; shake off excess.
Heat butter and oil in a large non-stick pan over medium-high heat; cook chicken 4 minutes per side until golden and cooked through.
Transfer chicken to a plate; add remaining butter and oil to the same pan.
Sauté shallots and garlic for 1 minute, then cook mushrooms for 3-5 minutes until soft.
Pour in Marsala wine and simmer for 3 minutes until reduced by half.
Add chicken stock, cream, salt and pepper; simmer 3-5 minutes until sauce thickens.
Return chicken to pan and simmer 30 seconds to reheat; garnish with parsley and serve.
Heat oil in a large pot over high heat; cook bacon until golden, transfer to a bowl.
Cook chicken in the bacon fat until browned; transfer to a separate bowl.
Cook mushrooms until golden; add to the chicken bowl.
Reduce heat to medium; melt butter, sauté garlic and onion for 3 minutes.
Increase heat to high; add rice, stir until partially translucent (about 1 minute).
Add wine, scrape the pot bottom; cook about 2 minutes until the alcohol smell evaporates.
Reduce heat to medium-low; add 3 cups of stock, stir occasionally until mostly absorbed.
Add remaining stock in half-cup portions, stirring between additions until rice reaches desired firmness.
Return chicken and mushrooms to the pot to heat through.
Add a splash of broth to create a soupier consistency; remove from heat.
Vigorously stir in butter and parmesan to activate the starch and create creaminess.
Serve immediately; garnish with reheated bacon, extra parmesan and parsley.
Mix softened butter, parsley, garlic and salt until smooth; spread onto parchment paper into a 10 x 6cm rectangle and refrigerate until firm, then cut into 2 batons.
Pound chicken breasts between plastic bags to 0.5cm thickness using a meat mallet.
Season chicken with salt and pepper, then place a garlic butter baton in the centre of each breast.
Fold the sides in and roll the chicken into a log from the bottom, seam-side down.
Wrap tightly in cling wrap, twisting the ends, and freeze for 30 minutes until fairly firm.
Coat chicken in flour (shaking off excess), dip in whisked egg, then press into panko breadcrumbs.
Freeze crumbed chicken for a further 30 minutes until fairly firm.
Preheat oven to 180C (350F) and place a rack on a tray.
Heat oil to 190C (375F) and fry chicken, turning occasionally, until golden (2-3 minutes) - it will still be raw inside.
Transfer to the rack and bake for 15 minutes until internal temperature reaches 65C (150F).
Rest for 2 minutes, garnish with parsley and serve.
Combine marinade ingredients in a large ziplock bag, add chicken and massage to coat thoroughly. Marinate for 24 hours (minimum 3 hours if pressed for time).
Prepare the lemon yogurt sauce and refrigerate for at least 20 minutes.
Heat a large non-stick skillet with 1 tablespoon oil over medium-high heat, or preheat a BBQ to medium-high.
Cook chicken for 4-5 minutes on the first side until charred.
Flip and cook a further 3-4 minutes on the other side.
Rest chicken loosely covered with foil for 5 minutes, then slice and pile onto flatbreads with lettuce, tomato, onion and yogurt sauce to build wraps.

ingredients.name: Chicken thigh fillets, cut into 2.5cm pieces

method_steps: Combine chicken with marinade ingredients in a bowl; marinate for a minimum of 3 hours or overnight.
Thread chicken onto 8 skewers.
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat (or use a BBQ). Cook skewers for 3 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve with tzatziki, flatbreads, lettuce and tomato slices.

For the tzatziki: cut cucumber lengthwise, scrape out watery seeds, grate coarsely, wrap in paper towel and squeeze out excess liquid. Mix with remaining tzatziki ingredients and let sit 20 minutes before serving.
Heat milk, broth and stock powder in a large saucepan over medium heat until gently simmering; add chicken and thyme sprigs.
Cover and simmer gently on medium-low for 15 minutes (avoid boiling to prevent the milk splitting).
Remove chicken, shred or dice, and reserve the poaching liquid.
Melt butter in a large pot over medium-high heat; add garlic and onion, cook 2 minutes.
Add thyme, carrot and celery; cook 3 minutes until the carrot softens.
Pour in wine, scrape the pot bottom, and cook 1.5 minutes until mostly evaporated.
Stir in flour and cook for 1 minute.
Add half the reserved poaching liquid, stirring until incorporated into a thick paste.
Add the remaining milk-broth mixture, parmesan and pepper; stir well.
Mix in the chicken and peas.
Once steaming, cook a further 3 minutes, stirring regularly, to thicken the sauce.
Transfer to oven-safe pots and cool in the refrigerator for at least 30 minutes.
Meanwhile, partially thaw the puff pastry and cut rounds 2.5cm wider (all around) than the pots.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F.
Brush pot edges with egg wash, drape pastry on top folding edges down, brush with egg, and cut a 2cm steam vent in the centre.
Bake 35-40 minutes until deep golden, then serve immediately.

ingredients.name: Chicken wings, cut into drumettes and wingettes

method_steps: Mix all marinade ingredients together in a bowl.
Place wings in a large bowl, pour marinade over, toss well, and set aside for 10-15 minutes only (do not exceed 1 hour).
Preheat oven to 180C (350F).
Line a baking tray with foil, then greaseproof paper.
Shake excess marinade from wings (reserve marinade) and spread on the tray in a single layer.
Bake 45-50 minutes, basting with a brush at 25 and 35 minutes using reserved marinade and pan juices.
Wings should be golden, sticky, and meat should pull easily from the bones with slight charring on the edges.
Sprinkle with garnishes and serve immediately over steamed rice.
Combine chicken with marinade ingredients in a bowl for a minimum of 20 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over high heat; sear chicken briefly (1 minute per side) until browned. Set aside.
Heat remaining oil; saute garlic and onion for 1.5 minutes.
Add reserved marinade, water, sugar and pepper; bring to a simmer.
Simmer 5 minutes, then add chicken smooth-side down.
Simmer uncovered 20-25 minutes, turning at 15 minutes, until sauce thickens to a syrup-like consistency.
If needed, remove chicken and reduce the sauce separately, then recombine.
Serve over rice with the glaze, garnished with green onions.
Place broth, creamed corn, soy sauce, Chinese cooking wine, ginger, garlic and cornflour/water mixture in a saucepan over high heat.
Bring to the boil, then reduce heat to medium and stir occasionally. Cook for 5 minutes until slightly thickened.
Adjust seasoning with salt, turn off heat, and slowly whisk in the egg so it cooks in ribbons throughout the soup.
Add the chicken, season with white pepper, and serve garnished with scallions.
Combine chicken, onions, bay leaf, thyme and red wine in a glass bowl. Refrigerate for 12-24 hours.
Strain the wine into a saucepan; simmer vigorously until reduced by half. Set aside.
Separate the onions and herbs from the chicken. Pat the chicken dry with paper towels.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F (160C fan).
Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Brown chicken thighs skin-side down for 2-3 minutes, flip and brown the other side briefly. Remove. Brown drumsticks about 5 minutes total. Set aside.
Cook bacon until golden; transfer to the chicken tray.
Cook mushrooms 5 minutes until golden; remove to a separate bowl.
Add oil if needed; cook onions 5 minutes until golden patches appear.
Add butter and melt. Add garlic, cook 1 minute. Add tomato paste, cook 2 minutes. Add flour, cook 2 minutes.
Slowly pour in beef stock while stirring until lump-free. Add the reduced wine.
Add chicken, mushrooms, bacon, thyme and bay leaf to the pot and stir.
Cover and transfer to the oven for 45 minutes.
Taste and add salt as needed; if the sauce is too thin, simmer uncovered on the stove until desired thickness.
Serve over mashed potatoes, garnished with parsley.

ingredients.name: Chicken breast, cut into thin steaks

method_steps: Combine garlic mayo ingredients and set aside for 20+ minutes.
Mix the seasoning in a flat dish.
Pound chicken to approximately 1cm thickness if needed.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over high heat.
Cook onions 5 minutes until golden; season and remove.
Preheat oven to 160C (320F); toast rolls and keep warm in the oven.
Heat remaining oil in the skillet on high.
Dredge chicken in seasoning; cook 2.5 minutes until golden.
Flip; top with cheese; cook 1.5 minutes until the underside is golden (cover briefly if cheese needs melting).
Remove chicken to a plate; cover loosely with foil.
Spread avocado on the roll base; top with chicken, onion, lettuce and tomato.
Spread garlic mayo on the roll lid; assemble and serve.
