Combine marinade ingredients in a bowl. Add chicken and coat thoroughly. Marinate 1-24 hours.

Heat 1 tbsp oil in a skillet over high heat until smoking. Cook the vegetables in a single layer in batches, charring and tossing, seasoning with salt and pepper, until softened and charred. Transfer to a platter.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in the skillet over medium-high heat. Cook chicken 3-4 minutes per side until caramelised. Rest 3 minutes, then slice.
Warm the tortillas. Layer with peppers, onion, chicken, sour cream and lime juice. Fold and serve.

132.new_title: Crispy Chinese Lemon Chicken - old school style! (RTE)
method_steps: Combine cornflour, flour, baking powder and salt in a bowl; refrigerate.
Pound chicken to 0.7cm thickness using a meat mallet; sprinkle with salt.
Dust each chicken piece with cornflour, shaking off excess.
Preheat oil to 160C in a heavy-based pot (minimum 4cm depth).
Remove the flour mixture from the fridge and whisk in cold soda water until just combined.
Dip chicken in batter, letting excess drip off, then place in hot oil.
Fry for 3 minutes until very pale golden; remove to paper towels.
Repeat with the remaining chicken; cool for 20 minutes.
Prepare the sauce: whisk cornflour with a splash of stock until lump-free, add remaining sauce ingredients, and simmer 3 minutes, stirring occasionally, until a thin honey consistency.
Heat oil to 200C; fry chicken 3 minutes until deep golden, turning halfway through.
Slice chicken into 2cm pieces and arrange on a serving plate.
Pour warm sauce over chicken; garnish with green onion and lemon slices, and serve with steamed rice.
Cut pork in half lengthwise to create two long strips.
Combine all marinade ingredients in a bowl.
Place pork and marinade in a container or ziplock bag; refrigerate 24-48 hours.
Preheat oven to 160C (320F).
Line a tray with foil and place a rack on top.
Remove pork from the marinade, reserve the marinade, and place pork on the rack.
Roast for 30 minutes.
Heat reserved marinade with extra honey over medium-high heat; simmer 2 minutes until syrupy.
Remove pork, coat generously with marinade, turn over, and roast 30 minutes.
Remove pork, coat with marinade again, turn, coat, and roast 20 minutes.
Coat again and roast 10 minutes until caramelised and sticky.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing.
Serve with rice and steamed Chinese greens.
Cut connecting tissue between fat and meat at 2-3cm intervals to prevent curling; lightly pound the pork.
Season both sides with salt and pepper.
Coat meat in flour, then egg, then breadcrumbs.
Heat oil to 170C and fry for 2 minutes, then flip and fry 1.5-2 minutes until golden.
Flip again and fry 1 minute until deep golden brown; drain on kitchen paper.
Cut each tonkatsu into 2cm wide strips.
Serve with shredded cabbage, tonkatsu sauce and steamed rice.
Boil water for the pasta and cook according to packet directions; reserve 1 cup of cooking water before draining.
Cut sausages into 1.5cm chunks.
Heat oil in a skillet and brown the sausage pieces on the outside.
Add onion and garlic; cook 2 minutes until translucent.
Add crushed tomato, chilli flakes, salt and pepper; simmer 8 minutes until thickened.
Add spinach, pasta and 1/2 cup pasta water; toss for 1 minute.
Serve with basil and parmesan.
Rinse and pat dry the pork shoulder, then rub all over with salt and pepper.
Mix the oregano, cumin and olive oil into a paste and rub over the pork.
Place pork fat-side up in the slow cooker with the onion, jalapeño, garlic and orange juice.
Cook on low for 10 hours or high for 7 hours until the pork is fall-apart tender.
Remove the pork, cool slightly, then shred with two forks.
Skim excess fat from the cooking juices and, if needed, reduce the juices down to about 2 cups.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large non-stick pan over high heat, spread the shredded pork out and drizzle with the juices.
Cook undisturbed until the juices evaporate and the underside is deep golden and crispy, then flip briefly to crisp the other side.
Remove from the pan and repeat in batches without crowding the pan.
Drizzle with any remaining juices and serve in tacos.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, boneless, cut into 3cm cubes

method_steps: Melt the ghee in a large heavy-based pot over medium heat, add the cinnamon stick, cardamom pods and cloves, and cook for 1 minute.
Add the onion and cook for 7 minutes, stirring often, until golden with browned edges, then add the garlic and ginger and cook for 1 minute.
Stir in the ground spices and cook for 30 seconds.
Mix in the tomato passata and salt, then add the stock. Add the lamb, stir, and bring to a simmer.
Cover and reduce heat to low-medium; simmer gently for 1 hour 45 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the lamb is tender.
Remove the lid and cook for a further 15 minutes to reduce the sauce slightly.
Stir in the yoghurt, extra garam masala and fennel powder, and cook for a few more minutes.
Serve with basmati rice and garnishes of coriander, shredded ginger and yoghurt.

ingredients.name: lamb shoulder, trimmed, cut into 3.5cm cubes

method_steps: Preheat oven to 180°C (160°C fan-forced).
Combine all the ras el hanout spice mix ingredients in a bowl and set aside.
Toss the lamb with salt and pepper. Heat oil in a large oven-proof pot over high heat and brown the lamb in 3 batches, about 3 minutes per batch, then remove to a bowl.
Reduce heat to medium-high, cook the onion and garlic for 3 minutes until softened, then add the tomato paste, ginger, cinnamon sticks and spice mix, stirring constantly for 1.5 minutes.
Add the chicken stock and water, stir, then return the lamb to the pot and bring to a simmer.
Cover and bake for 45 minutes.
Stir in the apricots, cover and return to the oven for 1 hour, checking halfway to top up with a little water if needed.
The lamb should be fork-tender; gently stir through the lemon zest.
Serve over couscous, topped with toasted almonds and fresh coriander.

144.new_title: Slow Cooked Lamb Shanks in Red Wine Sauce (RTE)
method_steps: Preheat oven to 180°C/350°F.
Pat the lamb shanks dry and season with salt and pepper.
Heat 2 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over high heat and sear the shanks in batches until browned, about 5 minutes, then remove and set aside.
Reduce heat to medium-low, add the remaining oil, onion and garlic, and cook for 2 minutes.
Add the carrot and celery and cook for 5 minutes until softened.
Add the red wine, stock, crushed tomatoes, tomato paste, thyme and bay leaves, and stir to combine.
Return the shanks to the pot, mostly submerged in the liquid.
Cover and transfer to the oven for 2 hours.
Remove the lid and cook for a further 30 minutes, until the meat is tender.
Remove the shanks and keep warm; discard the bay leaves and thyme.
Strain the sauce into a bowl, pressing on the vegetables, then return to the pot and simmer if needed to reach the desired consistency.
Serve the shanks over mashed potato with the sauce spooned over, garnished with fresh thyme.

145.new_title: Lamb Chops with Rosemary Gravy (loin chops, forequarter, cutlets) (RTE)
method_steps: Combine the olive oil, garlic, rosemary, salt and pepper, and coat the lamb cutlets. Marinate for at least 1 hour (or up to overnight).
Remove the lamb from the fridge 30 minutes before cooking.
Heat a large skillet over high heat (no oil needed). Scrape off excess garlic, then cook the cutlets for about 3 minutes on the first side and 2 minutes on the second for medium-rare.
Remove to a plate, cover loosely with foil and rest for 3 minutes.
Pour off all but 3 tbsp of the fat from the pan, return to medium heat, then stir in the flour and cook for 30 seconds.
Pour in the beef stock while stirring constantly to dissolve the flour, then add the chopped rosemary and a sprig, and simmer for 2-3 minutes until gravy consistency.
Meanwhile, place the couscous in a heatproof bowl, pour over boiling stock, cover and stand for 5 minutes, then fluff with a fork and stir through a drizzle of olive oil and salt.
Serve the cutlets and gravy over the couscous.

146.new_title: Tray Bake Dinner: Lamb Kofta Meatballs (RTE)
method_steps: Preheat oven to 200°C/400°F (180°C fan-forced).
Combine the lamb mince, grated onion, breadcrumbs, garlic, coriander and all the spices, and roll into 16 meatballs.
Toss the capsicum and red onion wedges with olive oil, salt and pepper, and scatter around the meatballs on the tray.
Spray the meatballs with oil and bake for 15 minutes.
Switch the oven to the grill/broiler on high and cook for a further 5 minutes.
Sprinkle with coriander and serve with lemon yoghurt sauce.
Preheat oven to 220°C/430°F (200°C fan).
Place the panko breadcrumbs in a bowl and grate the onion over them, including the juices, then mix well.
Add the garlic, shallots, dill, eggs, parmesan, salt and pepper, and mix thoroughly.
Gently fold in the salmon, leaving visible flakes.
Scoop 1/4 cup of the mixture per patty and shape into rounds about 1.5cm thick.
Drizzle oil over a baking tray and heat in the oven for 2 minutes until the oil spreads.
Place the patties on the tray without pressing down, and spray the tops with oil.
Bake for 15 minutes, flip, spray again, and bake for a further 5 minutes.
Serve with sour cream or yoghurt for dipping.

152.new_title: Panang Curry – Real Deal, From Scratch (RTE)
method_steps: Peel and devein the prawns, reserving the heads and shells. Simmer the chicken stock, add the prawn heads and shells, and simmer on low for 15 minutes, crushing the heads lightly and skimming off any scum.
Strain the stock into a jug and leave undisturbed to let the sediment settle.
Halve the dried chillies, deseed, then soak in boiling water for 30 minutes and drain, reserving the soaking liquid.
Blitz the peanuts until finely ground, then add the drained chillies and remaining curry paste ingredients with a little reserved chilli soaking liquid, and blend until smooth.
Heat the oil in a large deep frying pan over medium-low heat and cook the curry paste for 5 minutes, stirring constantly, until darker and aromatic.
Add the clear prawn stock, coconut cream, sugar, fish sauce and salt, and stir in the green beans.
Simmer for 2 minutes until the sauce thickens and the beans are half cooked.
Add the prawns and simmer for a further 2 minutes until cooked, then stir through the Thai basil leaves.
Serve over jasmine rice, garnished with chopped peanuts, chilli and extra Thai basil.
Cut the potatoes into chip shapes, rinse and pat dry, then double-fry in batches at 160°C until soft, and again at 190°C until golden and crisp; drain and season with salt.
Cut the fish into batons or fillets and pat dry.
Heat the oil to 190°C/375°F in a large heavy-based pot.
Dust the fish pieces with rice flour, shaking off the excess.
Whisk the flour, rice flour, baking powder and salt, then add the ice-cold beer, mixing minimally (lumps are fine).
Dip the fish in the batter, letting the excess drip off, then carefully lower into the hot oil and fry for about 3 minutes until deep golden.
Drain on paper towels and repeat with the remaining fish.
Serve the fish and chips with tartare sauce and lemon wedges.

154.new_title: Prawn Tacos (Shrimp) – with Chipotle Lime Marinade (RTE)

ingredients.name: extra virgin olive oil, for slaw

method_steps: Mix the marinade ingredients and toss the prawns through; set aside for up to 20 minutes.
Combine the slaw ingredients in a large bowl, toss well, and set aside for at least 1 hour until the cabbage wilts.
Mix the sour cream and sriracha to make the pink taco sauce, adjusting sriracha to taste.
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat and cook half the prawns for about 60 seconds per side; transfer to a warmed bowl and cover loosely with foil, then cook the remaining prawns.
Warm the tortillas and serve with the prawns, slaw, pink sauce and lime wedges.
Boil salted water and cook the spaghetti 1 minute under packet directions; reserve a mug of pasta water, then drain.
Sort the seafood by cooking time: fish and large prawns first, then small prawns, then calamari.
Heat 1.5 tbsp oil over high heat; cook the fish and large prawns for 30 seconds, add the small prawns for 30 seconds, then the calamari for 1 minute, and transfer to a bowl.
Reduce to medium-high heat, add the remaining oil, garlic and onion, and cook for 3 minutes until translucent.
Add the white wine and simmer for 1 minute, scraping the pan bottom.
Add the tomato passata and sugar, and simmer for 2 minutes, adjusting seasoning.
Add the pasta, seafood and about 1/2 cup of the reserved pasta water; toss gently and cook for 1-2 minutes until the sauce thickens.
Serve garnished with fresh parsley.

156.new_title: Kedgeree – British Curried Rice and Fish (RTE)
method_steps: Bring the chicken stock to the boil and poach the fish for 8 minutes until flaking; remove to a plate, discard the skin, and flake into large pieces.
Measure out 1/2 cup of the poaching liquid and set aside.
Add the rice to the remaining poaching liquid, bring to a simmer, cover, reduce to low and cook for 13 minutes.
Remove from the heat with the lid on and rest for 10 minutes, then fluff.
Melt the butter in a large skillet over medium-high heat, add the cardamom and curry leaves, and stir for 30 seconds.
Sauté the onion and garlic for 5 minutes until browning.
Stir in the curry powder and turmeric, then the rice, tossing to coat evenly.
Pour the reserved stock over the rice and toss through, checking the seasoning.
Stir through the peas and most of the coriander, then gently fold in the fish.
Spoon onto plates and top with the egg halves and remaining coriander.

157.new_title: Whole Baked Fish – Snapper with Garlic & Dill Butter Sauce (RTE)
method_steps: Preheat the oven to 220°C/430°F (200°C fan) and line a tray with parchment paper.
Trim all the fins from the snapper using scissors, leaving the tail intact.
Cut 3 diagonal slashes on each side of the fish through to the bone.
Season the cavity with salt and pepper, then stuff with the sliced garlic, parsley, dill and lemon slices.
Tie the fish with kitchen string at three intervals.
Season the skin with the remaining salt and pepper, drizzle with olive oil and rub in thoroughly, including into the slashes.
Bake for 20 minutes until the internal temperature reaches 55°C/131°F in the thickest part.
Rest for 5 minutes.
Melt the butter over medium-high heat, add the minced garlic and cook for 45 seconds to 1 minute, then remove from heat and stir in the chopped dill.
Serve the fish with the garlic and dill butter sauce on the side.

158.new_title: Prawn/Shrimp Pad Thai (Spice I Am Restaurant) (RTE)
method_steps: Soak the rice noodles in lukewarm water for 1 hour, then drain.
Heat 2 tbsp oil in a wok over medium heat, cook the prawns until the colour changes, then remove and set aside.
Add the shallot and stir-fry for 2 minutes, then add the tofu, dried shrimp, fish sauce, palm sugar and tamarind puree, and cook for 1 minute until the sugar dissolves.
Add the noodles, vinegar and chilli powder, and stir-fry for 2 minutes.
Reduce the heat to low, push the noodles aside, add the remaining oil and the egg, then push the noodles over the egg and scramble it through.
Remove from the heat, add half the peanuts, most of the bean sprouts, the garlic chives and the prawns, and toss gently.
Transfer to a platter, sprinkle with the sugar and remaining peanuts, and serve with lime wedges and extra chilli powder.
Place the fish, red curry paste, coriander, fish sauce, lime juice and egg in a food processor and blend until minced into a paste.
Transfer to a bowl and stir through the rice flour and green beans until fully incorporated.
Measure 1/4 cup of the mixture per patty and form into patties about 1cm thick.
Heat oil in a skillet over medium-high heat, cook the patties for 2 minutes until deep golden, then flip and cook the other side for 2 minutes.
Drain on paper towel and repeat with the remaining mixture.
Cook the jasmine rice according to packet directions.
Serve the fish cakes with jasmine rice, sweet chilli sauce, coriander and lime wedges.
Bring a large pot of salted water to the boil and cook the fettuccine until al dente.
Melt the butter in a deep fry pan over medium-high heat.
Add the shallot and saute for 2 minutes until tender.
Add the cream, bring to a boil, then simmer for 3 minutes over medium-low heat.
Remove from heat and stir in the Parmigiano Reggiano, salt and pepper until smooth.
Reserve 1/2 cup of pasta cooking water, then drain the pasta.
Transfer the pasta and reserved water to the fry pan with the sauce and salmon; return to medium heat and toss gently for 1.5-2 minutes until the sauce emulsifies and coats the pasta.
Serve immediately, garnished with extra Parmigiano Reggiano and parsley.

ingredients.name: Prawns (shrimp), peeled and deveined, tail on

method_steps: Marinate the prawns with 1 tbsp olive oil and pepper for up to 20 minutes.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pan over high heat; sear half the prawns for 45 seconds per side.
Remove and repeat with the remaining prawns.
Return the first batch to the pan; add garlic and stir for 30 seconds.
Pour in the wine; stir until mostly evaporated (about 30 seconds).
Add the butter and lemon juice; swirl until melted.
Toss through the parsley; transfer to a serving plate with lemon wedges and bread.

163.new_title: Chickpea Curry with Potato (Chana Aloo Curry) (RTE)
method_steps: Heat oil over medium-high heat; saute onion and garlic for 3 minutes until translucent.
Add spices and stir for 1 minute to release flavours.
Add potatoes, coating thoroughly in the spice mixture. Add a splash of water if spices stick.
Stir in chickpeas, tomatoes, broth, and salt. Simmer 15 minutes until potatoes soften and sauce thickens.
Adjust seasoning; fold in green onions and parsley.
Serve over rice with a dollop of yoghurt.

164.new_title: Palak Paneer - Indian Spinach Curry with Cheese (RTE)
method_steps: Saute onion and spices in a large pot over medium heat for 3 minutes until softened.
Add garlic and ginger; cook for 2 minutes.
Add tomato and chilli; cook for 3 minutes.
Add spinach in batches with the water, stirring until wilted each time; cook for 10 minutes, stirring occasionally.
Remove half the spinach mixture, blend using a stick blender, then return to the pot.
Add cream and lemon juice; cook for 3 minutes.
(Blend step covered above.)
Pan-fry the paneer in ghee until golden, 1-1.5 minutes per side.
Gently stir the pan-fried paneer into the curry.
Serve over basmati rice or with naan.
Preheat oven to 220C (430F); brush eggplant slices with oil on both sides.
Bake 35-45 minutes until tender and browned; cool on trays.
Saute onion and garlic; add passata, canned tomatoes, water, oregano, sugar, salt and pepper.
Simmer 30-40 minutes until thickened.
Reduce oven to 180C (350F); spread sauce on the base of a baking dish.
Layer one third of the eggplant, one third of the sauce, a drizzle of oil, basil and parmesan; repeat twice.
Top layer: add remaining eggplant, sauce, parmesan, then all of the mozzarella.
Bake 25 minutes until bubbly and golden.
Rest 5-10 minutes; garnish with fresh basil.
Prepare the sauce: heat oil and saute garlic, onion, bay leaf and herbs for 3-4 minutes until translucent; add tomato paste and cook 1 minute.
Add the wine and simmer rapidly until mostly evaporated, about 2 minutes.
Add the crushed tomatoes, stock, sugar, salt and pepper; simmer 20 minutes on medium-low.
Remove the bay leaf, blend until smooth, then simmer 1 more minute.
Stir in the basil; cover until needed.
Prepare the filling: squeeze the spinach dry and mix with ricotta, cheeses, egg, garlic, nutmeg and seasonings.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F. Spread 1 cup of sauce on the base of a baking pan.
Pipe the filling into the cannelloni tubes.
Arrange the cannelloni in the dish and pour the remaining sauce over, covering all tubes.
Bake covered with foil for 30 minutes.
Remove the foil, scatter parmesan and mozzarella over the top, and bake a further 15 minutes until melted.
Garnish with extra parmesan and basil before serving.
Peel and cut the pumpkin into 4cm chunks, removing the seeds.
Combine the pumpkin, onion, garlic, broth and water in a pot; bring to the boil.
Simmer rapidly until the pumpkin is tender, about 10 minutes.
Blend with a stick blender until smooth.
Season with salt and pepper; stir in the cream without boiling. Serve with crusty bread.

ingredients.name: Greek feta, cubed (meat-free substitute for bacon)

method_steps: Preheat oven to 180C/350F; line a 30 x 20cm pan with crumpled baking paper.
Grate the zucchini and squeeze out excess water.
Grate the onion, reserving the juices.
Warm the oil, gently cook the feta briefly, then add the garlic for a few seconds.
Whisk the eggs, then whisk in the flour until lump-free.
Mix in the zucchini, onion with its juices, oil/feta mixture and most of the cheese.
Pour into the pan and smooth the surface.
Sprinkle the reserved cheese and feta on top.
Bake 35 minutes, rotating the pan at the 20-minute mark.
Cool 10 minutes in the pan, then a further 5 minutes before slicing.
Heat oil in a non-stick frying pan over medium heat. Add onion and potatoes, cover with a lid, and gently stew for about 20 minutes, turning occasionally until soft and cooked through.
Whisk the eggs and season with salt and pepper.
Remove the cooked potatoes with a slotted spoon into a colander to drain excess oil.
Pour most of the oil from the skillet, leaving about 1.5-2 tablespoons.
Increase heat to medium-high and turn the grill/broiler to high.
Return the potatoes to the skillet, pour the egg mixture over them, and cook for 5 minutes until the underside is light golden.
If the centre remains uncooked, place under the grill for a couple of minutes until set.
Rest briefly, then flip onto a serving platter.
Serve warm or at room temperature, garnished with parsley.
Combine the quesadilla spice mix ingredients (onion powder, oregano, salt, cumin, paprika, pepper, cayenne) in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a pan over medium heat; saute onion and garlic for 2 minutes.
Add capsicum and cook for 2 minutes, then stir through the spice mix.
Add black beans, corn, tomato paste and water; simmer 3 minutes until thickened, then cool slightly.
Layer cheese, the vegetarian filling, extra corn kernels and coriander onto half of each tortilla; fold in half.
Cook quesadillas in a non-stick skillet over medium-low heat, covered, for 3 minutes until the underside is golden and crispy.
Flip carefully and cook uncovered for a further 3 minutes until crispy.
Cut into wedges and serve immediately with sour cream or salsa.
Heat oil in a nonstick pan over medium-high heat; saute garlic, onion and celery for 2 minutes, then add bacon and cook a further 2 minutes. Transfer to a bowl and cool for 10 minutes.
Mix remaining filling ingredients into the cooled mixture using your hands.
Lay a pastry rectangle with the long edge nearest you and brush egg wash along one long edge.
Shape one fifth of the filling into a tight log down the middle of the pastry.
Brush the pastry edge with egg wash and roll up with the seam side down.
Refrigerate for 1 hour if time permits.
Preheat oven to 180C (350F).
Cut each log into four equal pieces and brush with egg wash.
Place on baking paper-lined trays and bake for 30-35 minutes, swapping shelves at the 20 minute mark, until deep golden brown.
Cool slightly on the trays and serve warm with tomato sauce.
Mix marinade ingredients (except sesame oil); coat the beef, then stir through sesame oil. Refrigerate for 1 hour.
Soak black beans for 30-60 minutes; drain.
Heat oil in a wok over high heat; cook beef for 30 seconds until browned. Remove with a slotted spoon.
Discard most of the oil, keeping about 3 tablespoons.
Add black beans and stir for 20 seconds; add garlic and stir for 10 seconds.
Add capsicum and onion; cook for 1 minute.
Add beef back in and toss for 1 minute; pour cooking wine around the sides of the wok and toss for 30 seconds.
Pour in the sauce and stir for 1 minute until thickened and glossy.
Serve with steamed rice.
Preheat oven to 200C/390F (or 180C fan).
Spread panko on a baking tray, spray with oil, and bake for 3 minutes until light golden; transfer to a bowl.
Cut a pocket into each chicken breast.
Fold cheese in half and place 2 pieces in each pocket; repeat with ham. Close the pocket and secure with toothpicks. Season with salt and pepper.
Mix mayonnaise and mustard with salt and pepper; spread over the top and sides of the chicken. Sprinkle panko over and press gently, then spray with oil.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until golden and cooked through. Remove toothpicks.
Melt butter, add flour and cook for 1 minute. Add half the milk and whisk, then add remaining milk, mustard and parmesan. Cook for 3 minutes, whisking constantly, until thickened; season to taste.
Serve chicken with the Dijon cream sauce.
Mix enchilada seasoning spices together.
Heat oil in a saucepan; add flour and stir for 20 seconds.
Add 2 tablespoons of the seasoning, the stock and tomato passata; simmer for 4 minutes until thickened.
Coat the chicken with oil and seasoning; sear for 2 minutes per side, then rest and chop.
Saute onion and garlic for 1 minute; add pepper and cook a further 2 minutes.
Mix in refried beans, chicken, corn and water; cook for 2 minutes.
Preheat oven to 180C (350F).
Fill and roll the tortillas; place seam-side down in a sauced baking pan.
Pour the remaining sauce over the enchiladas and sprinkle with cheese.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden, then scatter with cilantro to serve.
Dry the pork skin overnight, uncovered, in the refrigerator.
Season the flesh with oil, salt, pepper and fennel; wrap in foil, leaving the skin exposed.
Roast at 140C/285F (120C fan) for 2.5 hours, retightening the foil at the 1.5 hour mark.
Remove from the foil and level the pork using scrunched foil balls to keep the skin horizontal.
Blast at 240C/465F for 30 minutes until golden and crispy.
Rest for 10 minutes before slicing and serve with steamed greens.

202.new_title: Pea and Ham Soup (slow cooker or stove) (RTE)

ingredients.name: ham hock (or bacon hock / meaty ham bone)

method_steps: Add split peas to the pot and nestle in the ham hock, then scatter in the garlic, bay leaves, onion, carrot, celery, salt and pepper. Pour the water over everything.
Bring to the boil, then reduce to a low simmer, cover and cook for about 2.5 hours (stovetop) until the peas have broken down and the ham is falling off the bone.
Remove the ham hock, shred the meat and discard the bone and fatty skin.
Remove the bay leaves, then partially blend the soup with an immersion blender in 2-3 short bursts for a creamy but still chunky texture.
Return the shredded ham to the pot and stir through; taste and adjust seasoning as needed.
Serve garnished with parsley, with crusty bread on the side.
Season each fish fillet with salt, pepper and a drizzle of sake; place on parchment paper and wrap in foil to form a parcel.
Bake at 200C/400F (180C fan) for about 12 minutes until the fish flakes easily or reaches 55C internally.
Unwrap the parcels, top the fish with green onion, ginger and chilli, then drizzle with soy sauce and sesame oil.
Heat the peanut oil in a small saucepan over medium-high heat until very hot, less than a minute.
Pour the hot oil over the fish and toppings so it sizzles dramatically; rest for 3 minutes before eating.
Steam the bok choy for 2-3 minutes until just tender and serve alongside the fish, spooning extra sauce over the top.
Mix the miso paste, sugar, sake and mirin in a bowl until smooth to make the marinade.
Coat the salmon with the marinade, skin side first then flesh side, wrap and refrigerate for 24-48 hours.
Scrape the excess marinade from the salmon without rinsing, and bring to room temperature before cooking.

Barbecue over medium-low heat, skin-side down for 3 minutes then flip and cook the flesh side for 2-3 minutes, resting covered in foil for 5 minutes; alternatively bake at 180C/350F for 15 minutes skin-side down, then broil briefly to caramelise.
Garnish with sesame seeds and sliced scallions, then serve.

ingredients.name: Cooked chicken breast, cut into thin batons

method_steps: Shake dressing ingredients (lime juice, rice vinegar, fish sauce, canola oil, sugar, garlic, chilli) in a jar and set aside 10 minutes for flavours to meld.
Place all salad ingredients (chicken, cabbage, onion, capsicum, cucumber, carrot, chilli, mint, coriander) in a very large bowl.
Pour over half the dressing and toss well.
Set aside 5 minutes to allow vegetables to soften slightly.
Toss again and add remaining dressing to taste.
Serve sprinkled generously with peanuts.
Combine cornstarch with 1 tbsp water until smooth; mix in remaining sauce ingredients.
Heat oil in a wok; stir-fry garlic and ginger, then add onion for 1 minute.
Add chicken mince; cook until no longer pink.
Add all vegetables; cook 2 minutes until carrot softens.
Pour in sauce; cook 1½ minutes until thickened and glossy.
Transfer to a serving bowl; arrange lettuce, peanuts and scallions separately.
Fill lettuce leaves with the mixture, top with peanuts and scallions, and roll to eat.
Mix marinade ingredients (fish sauce, brown sugar, lemongrass, lime juice, garlic); marinate chicken 15 minutes.
Soak vermicelli noodles 2 minutes in boiling water; drain and cool.
Combine peanut sauce ingredients (peanut butter, hoisin, vinegar, milk, garlic, sugar, salt, chilli paste) in a bowl.
Heat oil until very hot; cook chicken 3 minutes, stirring constantly until golden; cool on a plate.
Fill a bowl with warm water; submerge a rice paper sheet for 2 seconds.
Place the paper on a cutting board; add stuffing below centre (coriander, chicken, carrot, cabbage, mint, noodles).
Fold the bottom over the filling, fold in the sides, and roll firmly to seal.
Serve with the peanut dipping sauce.
Mix lemon zest, lemon juice, olive oil, mustard, grated garlic, salt and pepper; slather onto the top and sides of the salmon. Marinate up to 1 hour if time permits.
Preheat the broiler/grill to 280°C (525°F) or its highest setting, with the rack 20cm from the heat source.
Toss asparagus and tomatoes with oil, salt and pepper; arrange on a tray, leaving space for the salmon. Spray the salmon with oil.
Broil for 11 minutes until the salmon flakes and reaches 50°C internally.
Transfer to plates, grate parmesan over the vegetables, squeeze lemon over the salmon, add parsley, and serve immediately.
Combine chicken with the marinade ingredients (oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, pepper) in a bowl. Marinate a minimum of 3 hours, or overnight.
Thread the marinated chicken pieces onto 8 skewers.
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat (or use a grill). Cook skewers 3 minutes per side until cooked through.
Prepare the tzatziki: halve the cucumbers lengthways, scrape out the watery seeds, grate coarsely, then wrap in a towel and squeeze out excess liquid.
Mix the grated cucumber with yoghurt, vinegar, lemon juice, garlic, olive oil, salt and pepper. Let sit 20+ minutes for the flavours to develop.
Serve the souvlaki with the tzatziki, flatbreads, lettuce and tomato.
Combine the sauce ingredients (oyster sauce, soy sauce, sesame oil); set aside.
Heat 1 tbsp oil over medium heat; scramble the eggs, then remove.
Increase heat to high; cook the bacon until golden, about 2 minutes, then remove.
Add the remaining oil with garlic and ginger; stir-fry until aromatic.
Add the onion; stir-fry 2 minutes until golden.
Add the wine and sugar; simmer 20 seconds until mostly evaporated.
Add the prawns; stir-fry 1 minute.
Add the rice, sauce, eggs, bacon and most of the scallions; stir-fry 2 minutes.
Serve topped with the remaining scallions.
Mix marinade ingredients in a ziplock bag; add chicken and massage to coat.
Marinate for at least 3 hours, up to 24 hours, in the fridge.
Heat a skillet or BBQ to medium-high heat.
Cook chicken 4-5 minutes per side until charred and cooked through.
Rest 5 minutes before serving, sliced, in a bowl with rice and salad, or in wraps.
Combine marinade ingredients in a ziplock bag, massage beef to coat, refrigerate 2 hours to overnight.
Remove beef 30 minutes before cooking; pat dry with paper towels; discard marinade.
Drizzle 1/2 tbsp oil per side of beef.
Cook on hot BBQ or skillet to desired doneness.
Remove beef to plate; cover loosely with foil.
Sear onions in skillet with salt/pepper until soft and charred; transfer to serving plate.
Cook bell peppers similarly until charred.
Slice beef thinly against the grain.
Serve with warmed tortillas and desired fixings.
Preheat oven to 180C/350F (160C fan).
Puree chipotle in adobo until smooth using a stick blender.
Sprinkle pork with salt, pepper, and cumin.
Brown pork in batches over high heat in a large oven-proof pot (~1 minute per side); remove to plate.
Saute onion, garlic, and dried herbs & spices for 3 minutes.
Add tomato paste, cook 2 minutes; add chipotle puree, cook 2 minutes.
Add remaining ingredients except beans; stir and bring to simmer.
Place pork in pot, submerging as much as possible; cover and bake 2 hours.
Remove lid, add beans, return to oven uncovered for 30 minutes to caramelize.
Serve like a stew, in tacos, over Mexican red rice, or with tortillas.

ingredients.name: Chicken thigh fillets, boneless skinless (150g each)

method_steps: Combine marinade ingredients in a saucepan over high heat; bring to boil, then simmer until reduced by half (about 5 minutes) to syrup consistency.
Cool marinade, then combine with chicken; marinate at least 20 minutes (preferably overnight).
Heat oil in non-stick pan over medium heat; cook chicken first side about 3 minutes; flip and cook second side.
Before finishing, add 2 tbsp water to pan and coat chicken in the glossy sauce formed.
Serve with rice, donburi-style.

256.new_title: Korean Beef Bulgogi Rice Bowls - the easy way (RTE)
method_steps: Mix Bulgogi marinade ingredients in one bowl; combine drizzle sauce components in another.
Heat 2 tsp oil in a large nonstick pan on high. Add half the spinach, toss 30 seconds, then remaining spinach; toss until wilted (about 1 minute); remove to a bowl.
Add 1 tbsp oil to pan; cook beef mince on high heat, breaking it apart, until no raw meat remains; pour in the bulgogi sauce.
Simmer rapidly, stirring occasionally, until sauce mostly evaporates; continue stirring frequently until beef caramelizes but remains moist.
Spoon beef over rice; arrange spinach and carrot alongside; top with pickled ginger and a dollop of avocado; tuck lettuce down the side; sprinkle with sesame seeds and green onion.
Drizzle with sauce before mixing everything together.
Combine sauce ingredients in a bowl.
Soak rice noodles in boiling water per packet instructions; drain.
Heat 1 tbsp oil; cook shrimp until done (about 2.5-3 minutes); set aside.
Add beaten egg to make a thin omelette; roll, slice, and remove.
Add remaining oil; cook garlic, ginger, onion for 2 minutes.
Add capsicum for 1 minute.
Add noodles and sauce; toss. Include egg, pork, shrimp, and chillies; heat through 1-2 minutes.
Serve immediately.

ingredients.name: Chicken thigh, cut into bite-size pieces

method_steps: Mix cornflour and soy sauce; blend in remaining sauce ingredients except water.
Coat chicken with 1.5 tbsp of the sauce; marinate 10-20 minutes.
Add water to remaining sauce.
Heat oil in a wok; cook garlic, ginger, and chillies for 30 seconds.
Add chicken and white onion parts; cook until chicken turns white.
Pour in sauce and Sichuan pepper; simmer while stirring until thickened.
Mix in peanuts and green onion tops.
Serve with rice.

ingredients.name: chicken thigh fillets, skinless boneless, cut into bite-sized pieces
bird's eye or Thai chilli, deseeded and finely chopped

method_steps: Mix sauce ingredients in a small bowl.
Heat oil in a wok or pan over high heat.
Add garlic and chilli; cook for 10 seconds.
Add the white part of the green onions and the chicken; fry until just cooked, about 2 minutes.
Add the sauce; cook for 1 minute until the water reduces and coats the chicken in a thick, glossy sauce.
Toss through the green onion tops and basil leaves; stir until just wilted.
Serve immediately over steamed jasmine rice.

ingredients.name: chicken thigh fillets, skinless, cut into large pieces
bird's eye or Thai chilli, deseeded, finely minced
fresh coriander or green onion, to garnish
red chilli, finely sliced, to garnish

method_steps: Toss chicken with fish sauce and chilli; set aside while preparing other ingredients.
Use a large non-stick pan (30cm/12in minimum). Mix oil and sugar in the cold pan, turn to medium-high heat until the sugar melts, then remove from the stove.
Carefully add the chicken (it will sizzle), ginger, and shallots; toss briefly. The caramel may harden - this is normal and will re-melt.
Return the pan to the stove and stir until the chicken changes from pink to white all over, but is not browned or cooked through.

Add water, stir, and bring to a simmer. Simmer rapidly on medium-high (or high) for 10-12 minutes until the liquid reduces to a glaze. Stir occasionally while watery, then toss regularly once reduced for nice colour on the chicken.
Serve with jasmine rice or plain rice, garnished with chilli slices and coriander if desired. Pair with smashed cucumbers or Asian slaw.

272.new_title: Chickpea Curry with Potato (Chana Aloo Curry) (RTE)
method_steps: Heat oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and garlic; cook for 3 minutes until translucent.
Stir in the spices for 1 minute.
Add the potatoes and coat thoroughly in the spices, adding a splash of water if sticking.
Add the chickpeas, tomatoes, broth, and salt. Bring to a simmer; reduce heat to medium and simmer for 15 minutes until the potatoes are tender and the sauce thickens.
Taste and adjust salt. Stir in the green onions and parsley.
Serve with rice, yogurt, and coriander if desired.

ingredients.name: dark ale (beer) or low sodium beef broth

method_steps: Cut large beef cheeks in half to yield 12-15 pieces; if using chuck, cut into approximately 12 pieces.
Season meat generously with salt and pepper.
Heat oil in a large pot over high heat; sear beef in batches until deeply browned on all sides. Transfer to slow cooker.
Combine all Barbacoa Sauce ingredients in a blender, food processor, or Nutribullet; blend until smooth. Pour sauce over beef in the slow cooker.
Add bay leaves and arrange meat to submerge as much as possible.
Slow-cook on low for 8 hours until the meat shreds effortlessly.
Remove beef from the cooker into a pan; shred using two forks.
Pour 3-4 ladles of the cooking sauce over the shredded meat; toss to combine.
Use for tacos, burritos, enchiladas, quesadillas, or burrito bowls.
Combine all tagine spice mix ingredients (cardamom, coriander, cumin, fennel, cayenne, turmeric, cloves, ginger) and preheat oven to 180C/350F.
Heat 1 tbsp oil in a large pot over medium-high heat; saute garlic and onion until wilted, about 1.5 minutes; transfer to a bowl.
Add butternut squash to the pot with remaining oil; cook 3 minutes until lightly golden; remove to the bowl.
Add 2 tbsp oil, cook eggplant 3 minutes until tinged gold; remove to the bowl.
Add 1 tbsp oil if needed; cook capsicum and cauliflower 2 minutes until edges are golden; remove to the bowl.
Add tomato to the pot, cook 1 minute; stir in spice mix and cook 1 minute to bloom the flavours.
Return all vegetables to the pot; add water (should nearly cover vegetables), salt and pepper; bring to a simmer, then transfer to the oven for 30 minutes (or simmer gently on the stovetop).
Remove from the oven, add green beans and chickpeas; simmer uncovered on the stove for 15 minutes on medium-low heat to cook the beans and reduce the sauce.
Stir through lemon juice and zest.
Serve over couscous, rice or quinoa, or on its own, topped with yoghurt, toasted almonds, cilantro and a dusting of paprika or cayenne.
Mix yoghurt sauce ingredients (yoghurt, lemon juice, garlic, salt, pepper) and refrigerate.
Combine kebab ingredients (lamb mince, onion, garlic, pistachios, salt, pepper, cumin, chilli flakes) in a bowl, mixing gently (exclude the oil).
Divide the mixture into 8 portions and mould onto skewers.
Heat oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Cook the skewers for 8 minutes, turning to brown all sides.
Slice the tomatoes and onion while the skewers cook.
Serve with warmed flatbread, lettuce, tomato, red onion, and yoghurt sauce.
Combine chicken with marinade ingredients (olive oil, lemon juice, garlic, oregano, salt, pepper) in a bowl; marinate for a minimum of 3 hours up to overnight.
Thread the chicken onto 8 skewers.
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat (or preheat a BBQ); cook the skewers for 3 minutes per side until cooked through.
Serve the skewers with tzatziki, flatbreads, lettuce, and tomato slices.
For the tzatziki: halve the cucumber lengthwise and scrape out the seeds with a teaspoon, then grate coarsely, wrap in paper towel and squeeze out the excess liquid.
Mix the grated cucumber with the remaining tzatziki ingredients.
Let the tzatziki sit for 20 minutes before serving.

ingredients.name: Chicken thighs and drumsticks, bone-in skin-on

method_steps: Mix marinade ingredients; add chicken and coat. Marinate up to 24 hours if time allows, or proceed immediately.
Preheat oven to 180C (350F).
Place potatoes and onion in the baking pan, top with chicken; pour over the stock and marinade.
Bake for 20 minutes, then remove from the oven.
Scatter cherry tomatoes over, drizzle with 1 tbsp olive oil, and sprinkle with salt and pepper.
Bake for 30-35 minutes more until the chicken is golden and the potatoes are cooked.
Garnish with fresh oregano and serve.
Sprinkle beef with salt and pepper.
Heat 1.5 tbsp oil in a large casserole pot over high heat until smoking.
Add a third of the beef and brown aggressively all over, about 4 minutes; remove to a bowl and repeat with remaining beef, adding oil as needed.
Reduce heat to medium-high; add onion and garlic and cook 2 minutes until softened and golden-edged.
Add carrot and celery; stir for 1 minute to coat.
Sprinkle flour evenly across the surface, then stir to coat.
Add broth, red wine, tomato paste and Worcestershire sauce; stir to dissolve.
Add the cooked beef (with juices), thyme, bay leaves and potatoes; stir. The liquid should almost fully cover the ingredients.
Bring to a simmer, then adjust heat to low/medium-low for a gentle simmer.
Cover and cook for 1 hour 45 minutes until the beef is tender.
Remove the lid and simmer for a further 30 minutes until the sauce reduces to a thin gravy consistency.
Season to taste with salt and pepper, then serve.

293.new_title: One Pan Spanish Chicken with Chorizo, Tomato and Potatoes (RTE)
method_steps: Preheat oven to 180C (350F).
Combine rub ingredients and coat the chicken.
Parboil the potatoes until slightly undercooked.
Heat oil in a large oven-safe pan over high heat.
Sear the chicken skin-side down for 1-1.5 minutes, flip and cook for the same time; remove to a bowl.
Brown the chorizo for about 1 minute per side; remove to the bowl.
Add oil if the pan is dry (optional).
Saute the garlic, capsicum and onion for 2 minutes.
Add the canned tomatoes and oregano, scraping the pan bottom.
Return the chicken, chorizo, potatoes and cherry tomatoes to the pan; nestle the chicken half-submerged.
Cover and bake for 30-40 minutes until the chicken is cooked through.
Serve garnished with fresh oregano.
Preheat oven to 200C/400F (180C fan); fold parchment paper with 4 slits (about 10cm each) to create steam vents.
Heat oil in a metal roasting pan on the stove; saute the eschallots and garlic for 3 minutes until soft. Add the cumin seeds and stir for 1 minute.
Add the lentils, carrot, salt, pepper and stock; bring to a simmer.
Place the raw sausages gently on the surface (do not submerge); cover with the slitted paper, pressing lightly.
Bake for 25 minutes covered, then 20 minutes uncovered until the sausages are browned.
Rest for 5 minutes before serving the sausages with the lentils.
Heat oil; saute onion, garlic and ginger for 2 minutes.
Add the spices; stir for 1 minute.
Add coconut milk, tomato passata and chicken broth; stir until combined; season with salt and pepper.
Add the lentils and pumpkin; bring to a simmer.
Simmer for 10 minutes until the pumpkin is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Stir through the spinach until wilted, then add the cashews.
Serve over rice with coriander and chopped cashew garnishes.
Halve pork lengthwise, then slice thinly against the grain.
Combine all marinade ingredients (pear, onion, ginger, garlic, soy sauce, brown sugar, sesame oil, gochujang); add pork and marinate for 30 minutes or overnight.
Heat oil in a large non-stick skillet over high heat. Add garlic and onion; cook for 2 minutes until translucent.
Add pork; cook for 5 minutes until dark golden and caramelized, and cooked through.
Serve immediately with rice, garnished with scallions and sesame seeds.
Heat oil over high heat; cook garlic and onion for 1 minute.
Add capsicum; cook 2 minutes until onion is translucent.
Add remaining curry ingredients except spinach (chickpeas, coconut milk, crushed tomato, stock, paprika, cumin, cayenne, sugar, salt); bring to a simmer over medium-low heat.
Simmer for 12-15 minutes until the colour changes from pale pink to orangey-red.
Stir in spinach until wilted; add coriander and lime juice.
Adjust seasoning; serve over rice with yogurt and extra coriander.

ingredients.name: beef rump or fillet, thinly sliced

method_steps: Combine water and cornflour in a bowl, then mix in the remaining sauce ingredients (soy sauces, cooking wine, sugar, five spice, sesame oil, pepper).
Place beef in a bowl and add 1.5 tablespoons of the sauce; mix well.
Boil water in a large pot. Add broccoli and cook for 1 minute. Add noodles, separate with a wooden spoon after 15 seconds, then immediately drain (noodles in water no more than 1 minute total).
Heat oil in a large skillet over high heat.
Add garlic and stir quickly. Add onion and cook for 1 minute until lightly browned.
Add beef and cook until it transitions from red to brown.
Add noodles, broccoli, and sauce. Toss together for 1.5-2 minutes until the sauce thickens and coats the noodles.
Sprinkle with sesame seeds and shallots if desired, then serve.
